<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575700 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01489: Dr Deployment Schedule

- page_id: `249575700`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575700/Dr+Deployment+Schedule
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T07:42:27.445+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Deployed Resources viewpoint
- labels: ['dr-deployment-schedule', 'dr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Dr Deployment Schedule view addresses the roll-out of resource packages (new capabilities, organizational change, etc.) into individual organizations. This view shows the planned capability deployment into individual organizations for a set of Enterprise Phases. Each Enterprise Phase snapshot lists the Organizations and the Resource Package that it is planned to receive. Each Resource Package may be traced to the Capability it delivers. The Dr view may also specify the possible interactions between the deployed Capability so that planners can see at a glance which organizations have compatible systems, and when.

The Dr view may be used for:

- Fielding planning.
- Business change planning.
- Capability integration planning.
- Capability options analysis.
- Capability redundancy/overlap/gap analysis.
- Identification of deployment level shortfalls.

**Implementation**

the Dr view can be represented using:

- A Dr table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- A Dr spreadsheet report.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Capability Configuration
- Exhibits
- Actual Organization
- Actual Post
- Deployed Milestone
- No Longer Used Milestone
- Actual Project
- Enterprise Phase
- Whole-Life Enterprise

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576021 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01490: Dragging and Dropping

- page_id: `249576021`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576021/Dragging+and+Dropping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T12:28:16.758+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['drag', 'drop', 'select-drag-drop-rule', 'drag-and-drop']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Create relationships or assign property values by dragging and dropping one element onto another right in the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

There are a number of predefined rules to create relationships or assign property values automatically. You may also set up your own drag-and-drop specifications between any elements.

To select a rule

1. Do one of the following:
  - Drag a selected element onto another, hold it for a few seconds and then release it. A shortcut menu is displayed.
  - Right-click an element and drag it onto another (This action is valid only for Windows operating system users). A shortcut menu is displayed.
2. From the shortcut menu, select one of the rules you want to apply.

915612839

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A specific, required objective of the enterprise that the architecture represents.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| endDate : ISO8601DateTime[1] | The time and date at which the Phase ends. |
| startDate : ISO8601DateTime[1] | The time and date at which the Phase starts. |
| goals : EnterpriseGoal[*] | The Goal towards which this Phase is directed and is in support of. |
| visions : EnterpriseVision[*] | The Vision towards which this Phase is directed and is in support of. |
| statementTasks : EnduringTask[*] | Collection of statement tasks. |
| fulfills : Mission [*] |  |
| describedBy : ArchitecturalDescription [*] |  |

##### Extensions

UML Class

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: The overall aims of an enterprise over a given period of time.

DoDAF: (DoDAF::Vision): An end that describes the future state of the enterprise, without regard to how it is to be achieved; a mental image of what the future will or could be like.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. StV-1 Enterprise Vision](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/MODAF.+StV-1+Enterprise+Vision)

## PAGE 01499: Environment

- page_id: `249575740`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575740/Environment
- version_number: 1

- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 All Views Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1097168225

1097168227

1097168226

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A definition of the conditions in which something exists or functions. An Environment may be specified in terms of LocationType (e.g., terrain), Climate (e.g., tropical), and LightCondition (e.g., dark, light, dusk, etc.).

DoDAF: An object that encompasses meteorological, geographic, and control features mission significance.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

##### Related DoDAF views

[CV-2 Capability Taxonomy](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/CV-2+Capability+Taxonomy)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. StV-2 Capability Taxonomy](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/MODAF.+StV-2+Capability+Taxonomy)

##### Related NAF views

[NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/NCV-2+Capability+Taxonomy)

## PAGE 01500: Environment Property

- page_id: `249575741`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575741/Environment+Property
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:36:51.980+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 All Views Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1097184755

1097184757

1097184756

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A definition of the conditions in which something exists or functions. An Environment may be specified in terms of LocationType (e.g., terrain), Climate (e.g., tropical), and LightCondition (e.g., dark, light, dusk, etc.).

DoDAF: An object that encompasses meteorological, geographic, and control features mission significance.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

##### Related DoDAF views

[CV-2 Capability Taxonomy](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/CV-2+Capability+Taxonomy)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. StV-2 Capability Taxonomy](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/MODAF.+StV-2+Capability+Taxonomy)

##### Related NAF views

[NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/NCV-2+Capability+Taxonomy)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575934 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01501: Environment Selection dialog

- page_id: `249575934`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575934/Environment+Selection+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-03T08:53:35.328+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Dialogs
- labels: ['environment-selection-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Environment Selection** dialog opens when starting the UPDM plugin for the first time.

To select the enterprise architecture framework environment

1. Select the environment according to the architecture you are going to build: Read the brief descriptions of each enterprise architecture framework provided in the dialog (see the following figure). This information may help you decide what to choose.
  - DoDAF 2.0
  - MODAF
  - NAF v. 3
  - NAF 4.0
2. Click to select the Expert check box, if you want the user interface to be complex and have all details exposed. And if you are a new user, leave the Expert check box unselected.
3. Click the Continue button. The sample project corresponding the selected enterprise architecture framework will be opened (only if using Cameo Enterprise Architecture tool).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can change the selected environment and its mode via the **Select Perspective** dialog.

To open **Select Perspective** dialog

1. Click Options > Perspectives > Perspectives . The Select Perspective dialog opens.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <strong>Environment Selection</strong> dialog opens when starting the UPDM plugin for the first time.</p><p>To select the enterprise architecture framework environment</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the environment according to the architecture you are going to build:<br /><ul><li>DoDAF 2.0</li><li>MODAF</li><li>NAF v. 3</li><li>NAF 4.0</li></ul>Read the brief descriptions of each enterprise architecture framework provided in the dialog (see the following figure). This information may help you decide what to choose.</li><li>Click to select the <strong>Expert</strong> check box, if you want the user interface to be complex and have all details exposed. And if you are a new user, leave the <strong>Expert</strong> check box unselected.</li><li>Click the <strong>Continue</strong> button. The sample project corresponding the selected enterprise architecture framework will be opened (only if using Cameo Enterprise Architecture tool).</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:alt="Environment Selection dialog" ac:title="Environment Selection dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="environment_selection_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Environment Selection dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can change the selected environment and its mode via the <strong>Select Perspective</strong> dialog.</p><p>To open <strong>Select Perspective</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Perspectives</strong> &gt; <strong>Perspectives</strong>.<br />The <strong>Select Perspective</strong> dialog opens.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

##### Description

UPDM: Relationship between a Node and a capability the node provides.

MODAF/NAF: (MODAF::CapabilityForNode): An assertion that a Node is required to have a Capability.

DoDAF: A couple that represents the capability that a performer manifests.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Dependency

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576060 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01504: Exporting tables to Excel spreadsheets

- page_id: `249576060`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576060/Exporting+tables+to+Excel+spreadsheets
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-26T13:09:31.465+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Managing Tables
- labels: ['export-tables-to-excel-spreadsheets']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can export your active OV-3, NOV-3, OV-6a, NOV-6a, AV-2, NAV-2. SV-6, NSV-6, SV-10a, NSV-10a, SOV-4a, StV-5, CV-5, SvcV-10a, SV-7 Actual, SV-7 Typical, NSV-7 Actual, NSV-7 Typical, SvcV7-Actual, and SvcV-7 Typical tables into the Excel Spreadsheet.

To export a table

1. On the table toolbar, click the Export button.
2. In the Choose file dialog, specify the file name and location.
3. Click Save . The table is exported to Excel spreadsheet.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can export your active OV-3, NOV-3, OV-6a, NOV-6a, AV-2, NAV-2. SV-6, NSV-6, SV-10a, NSV-10a, SOV-4a, StV-5, CV-5, SvcV-10a, SV-7 Actual, SV-7 Typical, NSV-7 Actual, NSV-7 Typical, SvcV7-Actual, and SvcV-7 Typical tables into the Excel Spreadsheet.</p><p>To export a table</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, click the <strong>Export</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Choose </strong><strong>file</strong> dialog, specify the file name and location.</li><li>Click <strong>Save</strong>.<br />The table is exported to Excel spreadsheet.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exporting_table-to_excel.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Exporting tables to Excel spreadsheets" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576023 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01505: Exporting to PES

- page_id: `249576023`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576023/Exporting+to+PES
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T12:31:08.545+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['pes', 'export-model', 'export-view', 'export-viewpoint', 'export-to-pes', 'physical-exchange-specification']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Physical Exchange Specification is the specification for architectural data exchange.

You can export your model, view, or viewpoint to the Physical Exchange Specification (PES) file. This export makes it easier to work with architectural data of your model, view, or viewpoint in another environment (for example, you will be able to analyze exported data using spreadsheets or deposit information into repositories). The PES export is based on the DM2 and IDEAS Foundation XML schemas.

- You can find the IDEAS Foundation XML schema on the International Defense Enterprise Architecture Specification Group official Web site at http://www.ideasgroup.org/xsd/IdeasFoundation.xsd
- You can find the MD2 Foundation XML schema on the Department of Defense Chief Information Officer official Web site at http://cio-nii.defense.gov/sites/dodaf20/products/DM2Foundation_v2-01.xsd
- You can download the MD2 Domain XML schema at [http://metadata.ces.mil/dse/ns/ARCH/DM2_PES_v2.02_Chg_1.zip](http://metadata.ces.mil/dse/ns/ARCH/DM2_PES_v2.02_Chg_1.zip)

Elements and relations from the model, view, or viewpoint are exported to the selected location. The export is carried out using the QVT scripts. As MagicDraw supports QVT, you can easily modify the necessary script. You can find all related QVT scripts in <MagicDraw installation directory>\plugins\com.nomagic.updm\PES\qvtscripts\export. 
 You can download the QVT plugin from [http://www.nomagic.com/products/magicdraw-addons/qvt-plugin](http://www.nomagic.com/products/magicdraw-addons/qvt-plugin). For more information about the MagicDraw QVT plugin, see "QVT_plugin_readme.html" that can be found in <MagicDraw installation directory> and "SimpleUML_to_RDB_Sample_readme.html" that can be found in <MagicDraw installation directory>\samples\QVT. 
 
 To export a model

1. On the **File**menu, point to **Export****To**, and click **PES****(v2.02)****File**.
2. In the **Select PES File** dialog, specify the location and name of the PES file.
3. Click **Export**.

To export a view or a viewpoint

1. Right-click the view or viewpoint and select Export To PES .
2. In the **Select PES File** dialog, specify the location and name of the PES file.
3. Click **Export**.

- All exported data is stored in the XML file.
- After exporting to PES, predefined element ID's are not changed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Physical Exchange Specification is the specification for architectural data exchange.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> You can export your model, view, or viewpoint to the Physical Exchange Specification (PES) file. This export makes it easier to work with architectural data of your model, view, or viewpoint in another environment (for example, you will be able to analyze exported data using spreadsheets or deposit information into repositories). The PES export is based on the DM2 and IDEAS Foundation XML schemas.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="41147e4c-ca6e-41ed-8b29-db8712d222b2" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can find the IDEAS Foundation XML schema on the International Defense Enterprise Architecture Specification Group official Web site at <a href="http://www.ideasgroup.org/xsd/IdeasFoundation.xsd">http://www.ideasgroup.org/xsd/IdeasFoundation.xsd</a></li><li>You can find the MD2 Foundation XML schema on the Department of Defense Chief Information Officer official Web site at <a href="http://cio-nii.defense.gov/sites/dodaf20/products/DM2Foundation_v2-01.xsd">http://cio-nii.defense.gov/sites/dodaf20/products/DM2Foundation_v2-01.xsd</a><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span></span></li><li>You can download the MD2 Domain XML schema <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3a4b98c7-d4a3-41ce-87ab-f8dfa32ead15">at <a href="http://metadata.ces.mil/dse/ns/ARCH/DM2_PES_v2.02_Chg_1.zip">http://metadata.ces.mil/dse/ns/ARCH/DM2_PES_v2.02_Chg_1.zip</a></ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Elements and relations from the model, view, or viewpoint are exported to the selected location. The export is carried out using the QVT scripts. As MagicDraw supports QVT, you can easily modify the necessary script. You can find all related QVT scripts in &lt;MagicDraw installation directory&gt;\plugins\com.nomagic.updm\PES\qvtscripts\export. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> You can download the QVT plugin from <span class="nolink"><a href="http://www.nomagic.com/products/magicdraw-addons/qvt-plugin">http://www.nomagic.com/products/magicdraw-addons/qvt-plugin</a></span>. For more information about the MagicDraw QVT plugin, see &quot;QVT_plugin_readme.html&quot; that can be found in  &lt;MagicDraw installation directory&gt; and &quot;SimpleUML_to_RDB_Sample_readme.html&quot; that can be found in &lt;MagicDraw installation directory&gt;\samples\QVT. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To export a model</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">On the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>File</strong> </span>menu, point to <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Export</strong><strong> To</strong></span>, and click <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>PES</strong><strong>(v2.02)</strong><strong>File</strong></span>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Select PES File</strong></span> dialog, specify the location and name of the PES file. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Export</strong>.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></li></ol><p style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> To export a view or a viewpoint</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the view or viewpoint and select <strong>Export</strong><strong> To</strong><strong> PES</strong>.</li><li>In the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Select PES File</strong></span> dialog, specify the location and name of the PES file.</li><li><p>Click <strong>Export</strong>.</p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="3942c8b3-3949-4a79-afdb-6a45d553be1a" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>All exported data is stored in the XML file.</li><li>After exporting to PES, predefined element ID's are not changed.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

[NSOV-3 Services to Operational Activities Mapping](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/NSOV-3+Services+to+Operational+Activities+Mapping)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576024 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01509: Filtering Operational Activities and Functions

- page_id: `249576024`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576024/Filtering+Operational+Activities+and+Functions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-26T15:10:01.109+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['filter-options', 'filter-operational-activities', 'filter-functions']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to filter the activities and functions according to your needs in the:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Activities dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Producing and Consuming Activities** dialog .

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Producing and Consuming Functions** dialog.

****

**Filtering Operational Activities**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Filter Option | Filter allows the selection of... |
| --- | --- |
| Performed Activities | The Activities that are performed by the Node. |
| Show all Operational Activities composing performed Operational Activities | The Activities that are the composite parts of directly performed Activities. |
| Show only TOP most Operational Activities | TOP most Activities. |
| Show only atomic Operational Activities | The Activities that are composite parts of other Activities and do not have composite parts. |

****

**Filtering Functions**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Filter Option | Filter allows the selection of... |
| --- | --- |
| Performed Functions | The Functions that are performed by the Resource. |
| Show all Functions/ System Functions composing performed Functions | The Functions that are the composite parts of directly performed Functions. |
| Show only TOP most Functions | TOP most Functions. |
| Show only atomic Function | The Functions that are composite parts of other Functions and do not have composite parts. |

To apply filters

- Select appropriate options from the drop-down list.

For the more concrete element selection, use different combinations of these filters.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Activities dialog' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space='UPDM2P']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

315011046

315011048

315011047

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: An activity which is specified in context of the resource (human or machine) that performs it.

DoDAF: Activity: Work, not specific to a single organization, weapon system or individual that transforms inputs (Resources) into outputs (Resources) or changes their state.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| subject : ResourceInteractionItem[*] | The ResourceInteractionItem that is the subject of the Function. |

##### Extensions

UML Activity

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SV-4 Functionality Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/MODAF.+SV-4+Functionality+Description)

[MODAF. SV-5 Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/MODAF.+SV-5+Function+to+Operational+Activity+Traceability+Matrix)

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

471485503

471485505

471485504

##### Description

UPDM Artifact: The FunctionAction is defined as a call behavior action that invokes the function that needs to be performed. This concept is required for mapping the architecture with UML and does not have a DoDAF, MODAF, or NAF equivalent.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Call Behavior Action

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SV-4 Functionality Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/MODAF.+SV-4+Functionality+Description)

##### Related NAF views

[NSV-4 System Functionality Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/NSV-4+System+Functionality+Description)

## PAGE 01513: Function Edge

- page_id: `249575873`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575873/Function+Edge
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:14:42.153+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Systems Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Availability

472416544

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Availability

472416546

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Availability

472416545

##### Description

UPDM: An extension of «ActivityEdge» that is used to model the flow of control/objects through a Function.

MODAF/NAF: A FunctionEdge (MODAF::FunctionFlow, NAF::FunctionFlow) is a UML::ObjectFlow between Functions.

#### INFO: Availability

Availability

This has been extended in UPDM to additionally include UML::ControlFlows.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Activity Edge

##### Related DoDAF views

[SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/MODAF.+SOV-5+Service+Functionality+Flow)

[MODAF. SV-4 Functionality Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/MODAF.+SV-4+Functionality+Description)

##### Related NAF views

[NSOV-5 Service Behaviour Definition](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/NSOV-5+Service+Behaviour+Definition)

[NSV-4 System Functionality Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/NSV-4+System+Functionality+Description)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575710 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01516: Generating DARS report in A1

- page_id: `249575710`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575710/Generating+DARS+report+in+A1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-04T06:29:57.299+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint > A1 Meta-Data Definitions
- labels: ['dars-template', 'generate-dars-report-in-a1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before generating an A1 DARS report, you must load the DARS template in A1 diagram and provide the template with relevant data.

To load the DARS template in A1

1. Select the Architecture Description from the Containment tree and open its Specification window.
2. Click the Load DARS Template button to load DARS template. [ATTACHMENT filename='DARS_template.png']
3. Fill in the extension property values for the A1 DARS report. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Before generating an A1 DARS report, you must load the DARS template in A1 diagram and provide the template with relevant data.<span> </span></p><p>To load the DARS template in A1</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Architecture Description from the Containment tree and open its Specification window.</li><li>Click the <strong>Load DARS Template</strong> button to load DARS template.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="DARS_template.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Generating DARS report in A1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Fill in the extension property values for the A1 DARS report.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Architectural_description_specification_window.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Generating DARS report in A1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></span></li></ol><p> </p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">To generate DARS report use standard <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Generating document based reports" /></ac:link> procedure.</span></span></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575099 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01517: Generating DARS report in AV-1

- page_id: `249575099`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575099/Generating+DARS+report+in+AV-1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-04T06:29:41.823+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > All Views viewpoint > AV-1 Overview and Summary Information
- labels: ['dars-template', 'generate-dars-report-in-av-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before generating an AV-1 DARS report, you must load the DARS template in AV-1 diagram and provide the template with relevant data.

To load the DARS template in AV-1

1. Select the Architecture Description from the Containment tree and open its Specification window.
2. Click the Load DARS Template button to load DARS template. [ATTACHMENT filename='load_DARS_template_button_in_AV1.png']
3. Fill in the extension property values for the AV-1 DARS report. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To generate DARS report use standard [Generating document based reports](http://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDMP/Generating+document+based+reports) procedure.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Before generating an AV-1 DARS report, you must load the DARS template in AV-1 diagram and provide the template with relevant data.<span> </span></p><p>To load the DARS template in AV-1</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Architecture Description from the Containment tree and open its Specification window.</li><li>Click the <strong>Load DARS Template</strong> button to load DARS template.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="load_DARS_template_button_in_AV1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Generating DARS report in AV-1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Fill in the extension property values for the AV-1 DARS report.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Architectural_description_specification_window.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Generating DARS report in AV-1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span><br /><br /></li></ol><p>T<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">o generate DARS report use standard <a href="http://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDMP/Generating+document+based+reports">Generating document based reports</a> procedure.</span></span></span></span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576028 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01518: Generating document based reports

- page_id: `249576028`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576028/Generating+document+based+reports
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T12:37:12.420+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Managing Reports
- labels: ['create-report', 'quick-generate-report', 'report-wizard', 'generate-document-based-reports']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

MagicDraw report handling functionality allows you to store UPDM report data in a UPDM project. With this feature you can easily manage multiple reports with different properties and quickly print a report directly from the model. Another benefit from storing your report data within a project is that you can easily pass the report on to other users by passing the project. 
 UPDM reports can be handled similarly as any other artifacts: diagrams, matrices, and tables. There is a report data element created by default in a project template for each UPDM report. A report data element is filled in with predefined default data. 
 You can create a new report data element from the shortcut menu of a viewpoint or view package. 
 
 To create an AV-1 Overview & Summary report data element

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the All Views package.
2. Select Create Report > AV-1 Overview & Summary Information . A new report data element that stores the default data for a particular report is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The newly created report data element is filled in with default data for successful generation of the report. To see variables and data of the report, expand the report element node in the Containment tree.

If you need to change data stored within a variable, open the variable's Specification window and then edit the **Value** property value. 
 
 You can store not only variables as data within a report data element, but the editable default report properties as well. The following table lists available editable default report properties and their descriptions.

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Name | To specify the name of the report data element. |
| Generate Recursively | Set to true to generate the report, recursively including all inner packages within a defined report scope. |
| Data | To select one or more packages and / or elements to define a scope for generating the report. |
| Auto Image Size | To specify an image size in the report. |
| Empty Text | To specify a value to be inserted when data contains no value. |
| Image Format | To select an image format for all the images in the report. |
| Template | To select a report template.A report template is not part of a project. |
| Variables | To add, remove, and order report input variables. |

To edit a report property, open the report data element's Specification window.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
 You can generate a report directly from the report data element without using the **Report** **Wizard**.

To generate a report from the report data element

1. Right-click a report data element and click **Quick** **Generate** **Report**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Specify the location to save the generated report.
3. Select whether or not to open the generated report directly after the generation.

You can create several reports with different data for each type of the report.

You can also use the MagicDraw **Report** **Wizard** for report generation. Using this wizard, you can generate a report either from existing report data elements (after loading them to the wizard) or from external report data.

To open the **Report** **Wizard**

- Click Tools > Report Wizard and select the report templates for the UPDM views.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Using this wizard, you can generate a report either from existing report data elements (after loading them to the wizard) or from external report data.</span></p><p style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">To open the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><strong>Report</strong> <strong>Wizard</strong></span></span></p><hr /><ul><li>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong> and select the report templates for the UPDM views.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575656 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01519: Generating P1 Actual table from P1 Typical table

- page_id: `249575656`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575656/Generating+P1+Actual+table+from+P1+Typical+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T13:32:00.552+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint > P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['evaluate-measurements-in-p1', 'generate-p1-actual-table-from-p1-typical-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table' space='UPDM2P'] can be instantiated by creating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements table' space='UPDM2P'].

To generate a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements table' space='UPDM2P'] from a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table' space='UPDM2P']

1. Create a P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements Description table with at least one measurable Resource added.
2. On the table toolbar, click the Evaluate Measurements button. A new P1 Actual table is created.
3. Add actual values for all Actual Measurements in the P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements Description table. For each Measurement in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table' space='UPDM2P'] , an Actual Measurement is created and the row representing it is added into the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements table' space='UPDM2P'] .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="b7c62d8f-6bdf-48f2-b623-dbe954e9c74e" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 Typical table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> can be instantiated by creating <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 Actual table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<span> </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To generate a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 Actual table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 Typical table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><hr /><ol><li>Create a P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements Description table with at least one measurable Resource added.</li><li>On the table toolbar, click the <strong>Evaluate Measurements</strong> button. A new P1 Actual table is created.</li><li>Add actual values for all Actual Measurements in the P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements Description table.<br />For each Measurement in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 Typical table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, an Actual Measurement is created and the row representing it is added into the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 Actual table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

To generate a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-7 Actual table' space='UPDM2P'] from a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-7 Typical table' space='UPDM2P']

1. Create a SV-7 Systems Typical Measures Matrix with at least one measurable Resource added.
2. On the table toolbar, click the Evaluate Measurements button. A new SV-7 Actual table is created.
3. Add actual values for all Actual Measurements. For each Measurement in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-7 Typical table' space='UPDM2P'] , an Actual Measurement is created and the row representing it is added into the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-7 Actual table' space='UPDM2P'] .

A new SV-7 Actual table is created.</li><li>Add actual values for all Actual Measurements.<br />For each Measurement in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating SV-7 Typical table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-7 Typical table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, an Actual Measurement is created and the row representing it is added into the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating SV-7 Actual table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-7 Actual table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576067 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01525: Impact analysis relation maps

- page_id: `249576067`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576067/Impact+analysis+relation+maps
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-27T10:52:47.473+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Managing Traceability and Impact Analysis
- labels: ['impact-analysis-relation-maps', 'node-role-impact-analysis-map', 'node-impact-analysis-map', 'resource-role-impact-analysis-map', 'resource-impact-analysis-map']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Take your model to the next steps - perform the impact analysis. For this purpose, there are four predefined relation maps. Using these maps, you are able to identify the model elements which are affected by the changes of the context element.

The predefined relation maps for the impact analysis are the following:

- Node Role Impact Analysis Map
- Node Impact Analysis Map
- Resource Role Impact Analysis Map
- Resource Impact Analysis Map

Several predefined criteria helps you to determine how the context element is related to other model elements.

The Legend in the right-top corner shows you which color represents which criteria.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating predefined relation map
- Modifying criteria through the legend

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Take your model to the next steps - perform the impact analysis. For this purpose, there are four predefined relation maps. Using these maps, you are able to identify the model elements which are affected by the changes of the context element.</p><p>The predefined relation maps for the impact analysis are the following:</p><ul><li>Node Role Impact Analysis Map</li><li>Node Impact Analysis Map</li><li>Resource Role Impact Analysis Map</li><li>Resource Impact Analysis Map</li></ul><p>Several predefined criteria helps you to determine how the context element is related to other model elements.</p><p>The Legend in the right-top corner shows you which color represents which criteria.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="legend.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Impact analysis relation maps" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><a href="http://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM182/Creating+predefined+relation+map">Creating predefined relation map</a></li><li><a href="http://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM182/Modifying+criteria+through+the+legend">Modifying criteria through the legend</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576102 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01526: Implementation Matrix

- page_id: `249576102`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576102/Implementation+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T13:58:03.788+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['implementation-matrix', 'mapping-between-systems-operational-and-service-elements', 'nodes/performers-implementation-matrix', 'operational-activities-implementation-matrix', 'operational-exchanges-implementation-matrix', 'ov-sv-gap-analysis']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Implementation Matrix describes the mapping between Systems Operational and Service oriented viewpoint elements.

The DoDAF, MODAF, and NAF Operational viewpoint elements should be implemented by the Systems or service oriented viewpoint elements. The implementation in UPDM is defined by the Implements relationship connecting implementation and specification elements In order to specify the implementation and analyze implementation gaps the Implementation Matrix is added as a supportive product for UPDM.

**Implementation**

An Implementation Matrix in MagicDraw can be represented by a Dependency Matrix based diagram. The implementation elements will be used as the row elements and specification (implemented) elements will be used as the column elements.

To be more specific, systems elements are: System Resources, Functions, and Resource Interactions.

Operational elements are: Nodes (MODAF / NAF) or Performers (DoDAF), Operational Activities, and Operational Exchanges.

Service oriented elements are: Service Function.

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

**Predefined configurations**

There are several types of predefined implementation matrices:

- Nodes/Performers Implementation Matrix. It maps Nodes/Performers to System Resources only.
- Operational Activities Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Activities to Functions.
- Operational Exchanges Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Exchanges to Resource Interactions.

You can find predefined matrices by clicking **Analyze** > **OV-SV Gap analysis**.

These matrices provide analysis of the whole model of a particular implementation, so you do not have to define the scope or any additional properties for building them.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- System
- Function
- Resource Interaction
- Node
- Performer
- Operational Activity
- Operational Exchange
- System Function

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Implementation Matrix describes the mapping between Systems Operational and Service oriented viewpoint elements.</p><p>The DoDAF, MODAF, and NAF Operational viewpoint elements should be implemented by the Systems or service oriented viewpoint elements. The implementation in UPDM is defined by the Implements relationship connecting implementation and specification elements In order to specify the implementation and analyze implementation gaps the Implementation Matrix is added as a supportive product for UPDM.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>An Implementation Matrix in MagicDraw can be represented by a Dependency Matrix based diagram. The implementation elements will be used as the row elements and specification (implemented) elements will be used as the column elements.</p><p>To be more specific, systems elements are: System Resources, Functions, and Resource Interactions.</p><p>Operational elements are: Nodes (MODAF / NAF) or Performers (DoDAF), Operational Activities, and Operational Exchanges.</p><p>Service oriented elements are: Service Function.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:alt="Implementation Matrix" ac:title="Implementation Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Implementation_matrix.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Implementation Matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><p> </p><p><strong>Predefined configurations</strong></p><p>There are several types of predefined implementation matrices:</p><ul><li>Nodes/Performers Implementation Matrix. It maps Nodes/Performers to System Resources only.</li><li>Operational Activities Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Activities to Functions.</li><li>Operational Exchanges Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Exchanges to Resource Interactions.</li></ul><p>You can find predefined matrices by clicking <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>OV-SV Gap analysis</strong>.</p><p>These matrices provide analysis of the whole model of a particular implementation, so you do not have to define the scope or any additional properties for building them.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="dea06eaf-b519-435c-9eae-f05f7ce4521d" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="2bcf9555-1f9c-4da6-a8b5-9e9b1e3833a0" ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="55d28ffb-939e-409f-85c8-61b1989962d6" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>System</li><li>Function</li><li>Resource Interaction</li><li>Node</li><li>Performer</li><li>Operational Activity</li><li>Operational Exchange</li><li>System Function</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

DoDAF: NA

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576034 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01531: Inheritance, Aggregation, Composition, and Redefinition of Exchanges

- page_id: `249576034`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576034/Inheritance+Aggregation+Composition+and+Redefinition+of+Exchanges
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T13:34:24.801+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['inheritance-of-exchanges', 'aggregation-of-exchanges', 'composition-of-exchanges', 'redefinition-of-exchanges', 'aggregated-information', 'display-all-operational-exchanges', 'hide-inherited-exchanges', 'see-inherited-exchanges', 'redefine-operational-exchange']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Exchanges and Resource Interactions can be inherited, aggregated, or composed. You can redefine the inherited flows.

You can see the inherited, aggregated, and composed exchanges in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P']
- Role-based tables [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P'] , NOV-3, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P'] , NSV-6, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L3 Node Interactions' space='UPDM2P'] , and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P3 Resource Connectivity' space='UPDM2P'] .

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
**Case Study**

Let's say, we have performers *Search**Node* and *Place**Of**Safety* related with the Operational Exchange *Warning**Order*. The performer *Aircraft* specifies performer *Search**Node*. Then we draw an association between *Aircraft* and *Place**of**Safety*. In this case, the drawn association inherits the Operational Exchange information that can be realized or not and also can be redefined.

Let's realize the Operational Exchange. We can do this in two ways:

- By clicking [ATTACHMENT filename='display_all_operational_exchanges.png'] in the association's smart manipulator toolbar.
- By selecting the check box in the Realize column in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Operational****Exchange****Manager** dialog .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To hide inherited exchanges in dialogs

1. Click Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, select General > UPDM .
3. Set the Respect UPDM Information Flows on Generalization property value to false .

- Note, that the exchange is not created for the drawn association, it is only realized.
- The realization of the exchange will be removed from the association when Generalization or Exchange is removed.

All the inherited exchanges can also be seen in the role-based tables. The inherited exchange rows are displayed in gray. You can use the Inherited Operational Exchange or Resource Interaction option to see the inherited exchanges.

To see the inherited exchanges

1. From the active table's toolbar, select Show Rows and then click Inherited Operational Exchange or Resource Interaction button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

When we have the inherited Operational Exchange, we can redefine it. This means, that the inherited Operational Exchange will be copied together with the information such as Actual Measurements, Conveyed Items, Documentation. The new Operational Exchange will be created in the model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To redefine the Operational Exchange

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Operational****Exchange****Manager** dialog , select the inherited Operational Exchange and click the Redefine button. The new Operational Exchange is created.

All the information that comes from the inherited exchange when redefining it can be modified in the model.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Concept Role
- Node
- Performer
- System Resource
- Operational Exchange
- Resource Interaction

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Operational Exchanges and Resource Interactions can be inherited, aggregated, or composed. You can redefine the inherited flows.</p><p>You can see the inherited, aggregated, and composed exchanges in:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Exchange Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Resource /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<span> </span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span><ac:image ac:alt="Aggregation of information flow" Redefinition of Exchanges" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Case Study</span></strong></p><p>Let's say, we have performers <em>Search</em><em>Node</em> and <em>Place</em><em>Of</em><em>Safety</em> related with the Operational Exchange <em>Warning</em><em>Order</em>. The performer <em>Aircraft</em> specifies performer <em>Search</em><em>Node</em>. Then we draw an association between <em>Aircraft</em> and <em>Place</em><em>of</em><em>Safety</em>. In this case, the drawn association inherits the Operational Exchange information that can be realized or not and also can be redefined.</p><p>Let's realize the Operational Exchange. We can do this in two ways:</p><ul><li>By clicking <ac:image ac:alt="Display All Operational Exchanges" ac:title="Display All Operational Exchanges"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_operational_exchanges.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the association's smart manipulator toolbar.</li><li>By selecting the check box in the Realize column in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational</strong><strong> Exchange</strong><strong> Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:alt="Inheritance of Operational Exchange between Search Node and Place of Safety" ac:title="Inheritance of Operational Exchange between Search Node and Place of Safety"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Inheritance.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Inheritance, Aggregation, Composition, and Redefinition of Exchanges" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="cc237fa9-641c-4802-a43e-fcc177c0b73f" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To hide inherited exchanges in dialogs</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Options </strong>&gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the<strong> Project Options</strong> dialog, select <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>UPDM</strong>.</li><li>Set the <strong>Respect</strong><strong> UPDM </strong><strong>Information</strong><strong> Flows </strong><strong>on</strong><strong> Generalization</strong> property value to <em>false</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="7614ef55-b73c-44c6-ba9a-bda57482510b" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Note, that the exchange is not created for the drawn association, it is only realized.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">The realization of the exchange will be removed from the association when Generalization or Exchange is removed.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> All the inherited exchanges can also be seen in the role-based tables. The inherited exchange rows are displayed in gray. You can use the Inherited Operational Exchange or Resource Interaction option to see the inherited exchanges.</p><p>To see the inherited exchanges</p><hr /><ol><li>From the active table's toolbar, select<strong> Show</strong><strong> Rows</strong> and then click <strong>Inherited</strong><strong> Operational</strong><strong> Exchange</strong> or <strong>Resource</strong><strong> Interaction</strong> button.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:alt="Role-based table with inherited exchange" ac:title="Role-based table with inherited exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="inheritance_role_based.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Inheritance, Aggregation, Composition, and Redefinition of Exchanges" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>When we have the inherited Operational Exchange, we can redefine it. This means, that the inherited Operational Exchange will be copied together with the information such as Actual Measurements, Conveyed Items, Documentation. The new Operational Exchange will be created in the model.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Operational Exchange Warning Order redefinition" ac:title="Operational Exchange Warning Order redefinition"><ri:attachment ri:filename="RedefinitionOfInheritance.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Inheritance, Aggregation, Composition, and Redefinition of Exchanges" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br /></p><p>To redefine the Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational</strong><strong> Exchange</strong><strong> Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, select the inherited Operational Exchange and click the <strong>Redefine</strong> button. The new Operational Exchange is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="d8eb23ae-ce99-4912-b51e-a121df164715" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>All the information that comes from the inherited exchange when redefining it can be modified in the model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="ec712d9a-52b9-4176-a90c-ed4a6dfebf44" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Concept Role</li><li>Node</li><li>Performer</li><li>System Resource</li><li>Operational Exchange</li><li>Resource Interaction</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576039 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01532: Instantiating Structures

- page_id: `249576039`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576039/Instantiating+Structures
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-16T10:56:18.571+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['automatic-instantiation-wizard', 'create-instance', 'instantiate-proposed-typical-structure', 'typica-updm-element', 'actual-updm-element', 'instant-structures']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automatic Instantiation wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Automatic Instantiation** wizard to instantiate any typical structure of UPDM architecture automatically.

To open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automatic Instantiation wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select any number of instantiable UPDM elements and right-click them.
2. On the shortcut menu, choose Tools > Create Instance . The Automatic Instantiation wizard opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
 To instantiate the proposed typical structure

1. Select all typical classes you want to instantiate in a diagram pane or in the Model Browser.
2. Right-click on the selected typical UPDM elements and select **Tools** > **Create****Instance**.
3. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automatic Instantiation wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Automatic Instantiation** wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done. A new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space='UPDM2P'] Actual diagram is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

For instantiable UPDM elements corresponding actual elements are created. For example, by instantiating Organization results in Actual Organization and etc. For all other structural elements UML Instance Specifications are created.

| Typical UPDM element | Actual UPDM element |
| --- | --- |
| Project Type | Project (DoDAF), Actual Project (MODAF / NAF) |
| Project Milestone | Actual Project Milestone |
| Project Theme | Project Status |

| Post | Actual Post |

<strong>Create Instance</strong>.<br />The <strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> wizard opens.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OV-4 Typical.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Instantiating Structures" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To instantiate the proposed typical structure</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Select all typical classes you want to instantiate in a diagram pane or in the Model Browser.</p></li><li><p>Right-click on the selected typical UPDM elements and select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Create</strong><strong> Instance</strong>.<span> </span></p></li><li><p>Follow the steps of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Automatic Instantiation wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />A new <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> Actual diagram is created.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OV-4_actual.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Instantiating Structures" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>For instantiable UPDM elements corresponding actual elements are created. For example, by instantiating Organization results in Actual Organization and etc. For all other structural elements UML Instance Specifications are created.</p><p> </p><table><tbody><tr><td><p><br ## PAGE 01533: Is Capable Of Performing

- page_id: `249575757`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`

- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 All Views Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1102400360

1102400362

1102400361

##### Description

UPDM: Links a Performer to the behavior that it can perform.

DoDAF: The Performs (DoDAF::activityPerformedByPerformer) relationship is an overlap between a Performer and a PerformedActivity (DoDAF::Activity) wherein the activity is performed by the Performer.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF

##### Extension

UML Dependency

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575573 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01535: L1 Node Types

- page_id: `249575573`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575573/L1+Node+Types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:25:59.948+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l1-node-types', 'l1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The L1 Node Types view specifies the types of logical Node used in the architecture. The nodes can be organized into a specialization hierarchy (subtype-supertype). An L1 view is used to define all the Nodes that will appear in a Logical Architecture. Nodes are elements of capability that are assembled and orchestrated in Logical Architecture. The levels of capability provided by each node are expressed as Measures of Performance, and these may be dependent on the environments in which the Node is required to operate.

The L1 view may be used to:

- Initial set up of a Logical Architecture.
- Define measure of performance for requirements specification purposes.
- Define the types of environment in which Nodes may operate.

**Implementation**

To create an L1 view, you can use one of the following diagrams:

- L1 Node Types diagram. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

- L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

- L1 Free FormNode Types diagram.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L1 Node Types diagram' space='UPDM2P']Creating L1 Node Types diagram
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram' space='UPDM2P']Creating L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L1 Node Types diagram' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Node
- Logical Architecture
- Operational Exchange
- Exchange Element
- Node Port
- Capability
- Exhibits
- Actual Location
- Location Type
- Energy
- Materiel
- Is Capable Of Performing
- Operational Activity
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Problem Domain
- Security Domain

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575579 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01536: L2 Logical Scenario

- page_id: `249575579`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575579/L2+Logical+Scenario
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:24:42.218+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l2-logical-scenario', 'l2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The L2 Logical Scenario view shows, at a high level, the interactions between logical nodes and places them in the context of location(s). The primary purpose of the L2 view is to specify nodes (elements of capability) in context of each other. The context is usually expressed in terms of the information that flows between the nodes (e.g. the information flow requirements between capabilities in a given scenario). The context may also be flows of material, human resource or energy.

The L2 view may be used to:

- Define operational concepts.
- Elaborate capability requirements.
- Define collaboration needs.
- Associate capability with a location.
- Define problem space.
- Operational planning.
- Supply chain analysis.

**Implementation**

The L2 view can be represented using an L2 diagram which is based on theUML Composite Structure diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L2 diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L2 Logical Scenario diagram' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Operational Exchange
- Exchange Element
- Node Port
- Node Role
- Known Resource
- Needline
- Service Channel
- Service
- Property
- Request

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575583 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01537: L3 Node Interactions

- page_id: `249575583`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575583/L3+Node+Interactions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-12-11T12:32:47.228+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l3-node-interactions', 'l3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The L3 Node Interactions view addresses operational information exchanges between nodes. Information exchanges provide further detail of the interoperability requirements associated with the operational capability of interest. The focus is on information exchanges that cross the capability boundary.

Although the primary purpose of this view is to specify information exchanges, an L3 may also list flows of materiel, energy and human resources.

The L3 view may be used to:

- Define operational concepts.
- Elaborate capability requirements.
- Define collaboration needs.
- Associate capability with a location.
- Define Problem space.
- Operational planning.
- Analyze Supply chain.
- Secure models – e.g. domain-based security & entity trust models.

**Implementation**

The L3 view can be represented using:

- An L3 Node Interactions table. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- An L3 Node Role Interactions table. [ATTACHMENT filename='L3_node_role_interactions.png']

- An L3 spreadsheet report.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L3 Node Interactions table' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Node
- Node Role
- Operational Exchange
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel
- Operational Activity

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Information exchanges provide further detail of the interoperability requirements associated with the operational capability of interest. The focus is on information exchanges that cross the capability boundary.</p><p>Although the primary purpose of this view is to specify information exchanges, an L3 may also list flows of materiel, energy and human resources.</p><p>The L3 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Define operational concepts.</li><li>Elaborate capability requirements.</li><li>Define collaboration needs.</li><li>Associate capability with a location.</li><li>Define Problem space.</li><li>Operational planning.</li><li>Analyze Supply chain.</li><li>Secure models – e.g. domain-based security &amp; entity trust models.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The L3 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0491c704-9963-4cd5-96b5-de8ff82c5883">An L3 Node Interactions table.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="L3 Node Interactions" ac:title="L3 Node Interactions"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L3.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="L3 Node Interactions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul><ul><li>An L3 Node Role Interactions table.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="L3 Node Role Interactions table" ac:title="L3 Node Role Interactions table"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L3_node_role_interactions.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="L3 Node Interactions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6868edaf-6a78-4687-8f6e-47f84c345cd2">An L3 spreadsheet report.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="f4cffc66-6c5d-4563-a5d6-5fa00012a765" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating L3 Node Interactions table" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="89b81902-7c65-4732-a052-c0f6486597e1" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Node</li><li>Node Role</li><li>Operational Exchange</li><li>Exchange Element</li><li>Energy</li><li>Materiel</li><li>Operational Activity</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575587 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01538: L4 Logical Activities

- page_id: `249575587`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575587/L4+Logical+Activities
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:25:08.829+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l4-logical-activities', 'l4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The L4 Logical Activities view describes the operations that are normally conducted in the course of achieving a mission or a business goal. It describes operational activities (or tasks), Input/Output flows between activities and to/from activities that are outside the scope of the Architecture. An operational activity is a logical process, specified independently of how it is carried out. To maintain this independence from implementation, logical nodes in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Scenario' space='UPDM2P'] are used to represent the structure which carries out the operational activities. Operational activities are realized as functions (P4) which are the “how” to the Operational Activities’ “what”. That is, they are specified in terms of the resources that carry them out.

The L4 activity models describe the business processes associated with the architecture, as well as the:

- Relationships or dependencies among the business processes.
- Information exchanged between business processes.
- External interchanges (from/to business processes that are outside the scope of the model).

The L4 view may be used to: 
 • Clearly delineate lines of responsibility for activities when coupled with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Scenario' space='UPDM2P']. 
 • Uncover unnecessary Operational Activity redundancy. 
 • Make decisions about streamlining, combining, or omitting activities. 
 • Define or flag issues, opportunities, or operational activities and their interactions (information flows among the activities) that need to be scrutinised further. 
 • Provide a necessary foundation for depicting activity sequencing and timing in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L8 Logical Constraints' space='UPDM2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L5 Logical States' space='UPDM2P'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L6 Logical Sequence' space='UPDM2P'].

**Implementation**

The L4 view can be represented using:

- An L4 Logical Activities diagram for Operational Activity Flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='L4_flow_model.png']

- An L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='L4.png']
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Activity diagram.
- A SysML Block diagram.
- A SysML Activity diagram.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L4 Logical Activities diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L4 Logical Activities diagram' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Operational Activity
- Operational Parameter
- Standard Operational Activity
- Node
- Is Capable Of Performing
- Operational Activity Action
- Operational Activity Edge
- Operational Exchange
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575593 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01539: L5 Logical States

- page_id: `249575593`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575593/L5+Logical+States
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:25:40.300+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l5-logical-states', 'l5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The L5 Logical States view describes how operational nodes or activities respond to various events by changing state. The view represents the sets of events to which the architecture will respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

The L5 view can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. The L5 is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L4 Logical Activities' space='UPDM2P'].

**Implementation**

The L5 view can be represented using an UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Operational State Description
- Operational State
- Node

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The L5 Logical States view describes how operational nodes or activities respond to various events by changing state. The view represents the sets of events to which the architecture will respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.</p><p>The L5 view can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. The L5 is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="L4 Logical Activities" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L4 - Logical Activities]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The L5 view can be represented using an UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="L5 Logical States" ac:title="L5 Logical States"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L5.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="L5 Logical States" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational State Description</li><li>Operational State</li><li>Node</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````
