<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575595 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01540: L6 Logical Sequence

- page_id: `249575595`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575595/L6+Logical+Sequence
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:26:37.889+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l6-logical-sequence', 'l6']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The L6 Logical Sequence view provides a time-ordered examination of the exchanges between participating Operational Nodes as a result of a particular scenario. Each event-trace diagram will have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

Operational Event-Trace Descriptions, sometimes called sequence diagrams, event scenarios or timing diagrams, allow the tracing of interactions between nodes in a scenario or critical sequence of events. The node interactions may correspond to flows of information, energy, material or people specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Scenario' space='UPDM2P']. The L6 can be used by itself or in conjunction with an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L5 Logical States' space='UPDM2P'] to describe the dynamic behavior of nodes. The diagram below shows the components of an L6. The items across the top of the diagram are operational nodes.

Each node has a vertical time-line associated with it. Specific points in time can be labeled running down the left-hand side of the diagram. Directed lines between the node time lines represent interactions (e.g. information exchanges) between nodes, and the points at which they intersect the timeliness represent the times at which the nodes become aware of the events.

The L6 view may be used to:

- Analyze operational events.
- Sequences of interactions between nodes.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identify non-functional user requirements (input to URD).
- Operational test scenarios.

**Implementation**

The L6 view can be represented using a L6 diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L6 Logical Sequence diagram' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Operational Event Trace
- Operational Message
- Operational Exchange
- Node
- Node Role

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The L6 can be used by itself or in conjunction with an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="L5 Logical States" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L5 - Logical States]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to describe the dynamic behavior of nodes. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575598 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01541: L7 Logical Data Model

- page_id: `249575598`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575598/L7+Logical+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:26:52.890+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l7-logical-data-model', 'l7']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The L7 Logical Data Model view addresses the information perspective on an operational architecture. The L7 view is used to document the business information requirements and structural business process rules of the architecture. It describes the information that is associated with the information exchanges of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.

The L7 view may be used for:

- Information architecture.
- Information product hierarchy.

**Implementation**

The L7 view can be represented using:

- An L7 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='L7.png']
- A UML Class diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L7 diagram' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Logical Data Model
- Entity Item
- Entity Attribute
- Entity Relationship
- Details
- Exchange Element

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.</p><p>The L7 view may be used for:</p><ul><li>Information architecture.</li><li>Information product hierarchy.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The L7 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li>An L7 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="L7 Logical Data Model diagram" ac:title="L7 Logical Data Model diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L7.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="L7 Logical Data Model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="10edf244-13a3-4847-ab2b-09818f2475aa">A UML Class diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="10edf244-13a3-4847-ab2b-09818f2475aa">A SysML Block Definition diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating L7 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Logical Data Model</li><li>Entity Item</li><li>Entity Attribute</li><li>Entity Relationship</li><li>Details</li><li>Exchange Element</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575601 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01542: L8 Logical Constraints

- page_id: `249575601`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575601/L8+Logical+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:27:43.589+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l8-logical-constraints', 'l8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The L8 Operational Rules Model specifies operational or business rules that are constraints on the way that business is done in the Enterprise.

This view is used to constrain the logical architecture without forcing a particular solution. The L8 view is used for rules which are not expressed as behavioral models, interactions or measures of effectiveness – i.e. they are textual statements of requirement that constrain the architecture.

The L8 view may be used to:

- Define doctrinally correct operational procedures.
- Define business rules.
- Identify operational constraints.

**Implementation**

The L8 view can be represented using:

- An L8 table. [ATTACHMENT filename='L8.PNG']
- An L8 spreadsheet report.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L8 table' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Operational Constraint
- Node
- Operational Activity
- Entity Item
- Operational Exchange
- Exchange Element

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The L8 Operational Rules Model specifies operational or business rules that are constraints on the way that business is done in the Enterprise.</p><p>This view is used to constrain the logical architecture without forcing a particular solution. The L8 view is used for rules which are not expressed as behavioral models, interactions or measures of effectiveness – i.e. they are textual statements of requirement that constrain the architecture.</p><p>The L8 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Define doctrinally correct operational procedures.</li><li>Define business rules.</li><li>Identify operational constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The L8 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="717c4ec6-4d7e-4b2a-b740-fa6f77c40d40">An L8 table.<br /></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:image ac:alt="L8 Logical Constraints table" ac:height="331" ac:title="L8 Logical Constraints table" ac:width="627"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L8.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="L8 Logical Constraints" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="717c4ec6-4d7e-4b2a-b740-fa6f77c40d40"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c8891382-42df-48f5-9219-93a881eb0a3d">An L8 spreadsheet report.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating L8 table" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Constraint</li><li>Node</li><li>Operational Activity</li><li>Entity Item</li><li>Operational Exchange</li><li>Exchange Element</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

1102061236

1102061239

1102061238

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A general specification of the surroundings / scenario in which an operation may take place. Examples would be: “desert”, “arctic”, “at sea”, etc.

DoDAF: A point or extent in space that may be referred to physically or logically. Includes concepts such as: Facility, Installation, RealProperty, Site,, and instances of conditions such as underwater (as specified in UJTLs).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Data Type

##### Related DoDAF views

[CV-2 Capability Taxonomy](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/CV-2+Capability+Taxonomy)

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1096998336

1096998339

1096998338

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A LogicalDataModel is a specification of business information requirements as a formal data structure, where relationships and classes (entities) are used to specify the logic which underpins the information.

DoDAF: A Logical Data Model allows analysis of an architecture’s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation specific or product specific issues.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Package

##### Related DoDAF views

[DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/DIV-1+Conceptual+Data+Model)

[DIV-2 Logical Data Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/DIV-2+Logical+Data+Model)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. OV-7 Information Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/MODAF.+OV-7+Information+Model)

##### Related NAF views

[NOV-7 Information Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/NOV-7+Information+Model)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575572 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01548: Logical viewpoint

- page_id: `249575572`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575572/Logical+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-13T10:10:58.144+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['logical-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Logical viewpoint is a description of the tasks and activities, operational elements, and information exchanges required to accomplish NATO missions. It contains graphical and textual products that comprise an identification of the nodes, their assigned tasks and activities, and dependencies between nodes. The Logical viewpoint defines the types of information exchanged, which tasks and activities are supported by the information exchanges, and the operational details of information exchanges.

#### PANEL: Views

Views

1

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It contains graphical and textual products that comprise an identification of the nodes, their assigned tasks and activities, and dependencies between nodes. The <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Logical viewpoint</span> defines the types of information exchanged, which tasks and activities are supported by the information exchanges, and the operational details of information exchanges.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Views</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children"><ac:parameter ac:name="depth">1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575604 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01549: Lr Lines of Development

- page_id: `249575604`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575604/Lr+Lines+of+Development
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T11:33:46.939+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['lr-lines-of-development', 'lr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Lr Lines of Development view specifies the logical threads (lines of development) for a set of projects and programmes. The status of each of these threads can be shown at the various milestones in the projects or programmes. The Lr view is primarily intended to support the acquisition process across multiple projects or programmes, including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of all the DLODs to achieve a successfully integrated military capability.

Use of the Lr view should support the management of capability delivery and be aligned with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cr Capability Roadmap' space='UPDM2P']Capability Roadmapview.

The Lr view may be used for:

- Project management and control (including delivery timescales).
- Project dependencies and the identification of associated risk.
- Portfolio management.
- Through Life Management Planning (TLMP).

**Implementation**

The L3 view can be represented using:

- Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix . [ATTACHMENT filename='Lr_program_to_capability.png']
- Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='Lr_program_portfolio.png']

- Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart). [ATTACHMENT filename='Lr_programme_to_capability_mapping.png']
- Lr Responsibility Matrix. [ATTACHMENT filename='Lr_matrix.png']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix' space='UPDM2P']Building Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Lr Responsibility Matrix' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- [Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)](http://docs.nomagic.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=2064481)

****

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Project
- Activity Part Of Capability
- Activity Part Of Project
- Actual Organization
- Actual Post
- Actual Project
- Actual Project Milestone
- Actual Project Milestone Role
- Increment Milestone
- Milestone Sequence
- Organizational Project Relationship
- Out Of Service Milestone
- Project Milestone
- Project Milestone Role
- Project Ownership
- Project Sequence
- Project Sequence
- Project Status
- Project Theme
- Project Type
- Status Indicators

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576007 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01551: Managing Project Properties

- page_id: `249576007`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576007/Managing+Project+Properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-08T12:09:12.461+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['architectural-description-element', 'root-element', 'manage-project-properties']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All project properties are stored in the Architectural Description element which is also the root element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can view, add, or edit project properties in the Specification window of the Architectural Description.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575248 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01553: Managing Resources in SvcV-8 diagram

- page_id: `249575248`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575248/Managing+Resources+in+SvcV-8+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-18T10:45:00.648+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description
- labels: ['assign-resources-in-svcv-8', 'remove-resources-in-svcv-8', 'manage-resources-in-svcv-8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before assigning Resources, make sure they have Increment and Out of Service milestones specified. If the Resource does not have milestones specified, you are able to see it in the SvcV-8 diagram.

To assign a Resource, do either

- Right-click the Version of Configuration and select Resource . From the Select Resource dialog choose the desired Resource and click OK .
- Select a Resource in the Containment tree and drag it to the Version of Configuration

To remove a Resource

1. Right-click the Resource bar and from the shortcut menu, select Remove Resource > <name of the resource you want to remove>.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Before assigning Resources, make sure they have Increment and Out of Service milestones specified. If the Resource does not have milestones specified, you are able to see it in the SvcV-8 diagram.</p><p>To assign a Resource, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click the Version of Configuration and select <strong>Resource</strong>. From the <strong>Select Resource</strong> dialog choose the desired Resource and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Select a Resource in the Containment tree and drag it to the Version of Configuration<p> </p></li></ul><div class="wiki-content"><p>To remove a Resource</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Resource bar and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Remove Resource</strong> &gt; &lt;name of the resource you want to remove&gt;.</li></ol><p> </p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575272 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01554: Managing SV-3 matrix

- page_id: `249575272`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575272/Managing+SV-3+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-17T11:05:05.017+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix
- labels: ['add-resource-interaction-in-sv-3-matrix', 'delete-resource-interaction-from-sv-3-matrix', 'manage-sv-3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new Resource Interaction

1. Right-click the cell and select Add New Resource Interaction .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard .
3. Click Finish when you are done. A new Resource Interaction appears in the selected cell.

To remove an existing Resource Interaction

1. Right-click the filled cell.
2. Under the Delete section, choose the Resource Interaction you want to remove from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To add a new Resource Interaction</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the cell and select <strong>Add New Resource Interaction</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />A new Resource Interaction appears in the selected cell.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove an existing Resource Interaction</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the filled cell.</li><li>Under the <strong>Delete</strong> section, choose the Resource Interaction you want to remove from the model.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576054 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01557: Managing Tables

- page_id: `249576054`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576054/Managing+Tables
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-16T11:15:34.536+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['table-toolbar', 'data-table', 'add-new', 'add-existing', 'delete-from-table', 'delete', 'default-owner', 'move-up', 'move-down', 'show-columns', 'report', 'show-full-types', 'show-rows', 'manage-tables']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The purpose of the tables is to improve usability of particular UPDM products that cannot be achieved by using custom diagrams or matrices. Tables can be created from diagrams menu exactly the same as diagrams. All the tables in UPDM plugin are editable.

The table pane consists of two major parts:

1. Toolbar . It's position is fixed at the top of the table.
2. Data Table. It is right below the toolbar.

These parts are arranged horizontally.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Toolbar buttons are quite similar for all tables. There are the **Add****New**, **Add****Existing**, **Delete****from****Table**, **Delete**, **Default****Owner**, **Move****Up** and **Move****Down**, **Show****Columns**, **Report**, **Export**, and **Show****Full****Types** buttons on the toolbar. Other buttons that are specific to particular product are described within the product specification sections.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Add New | To create a new element in the model and a new row in the data table. |
| Add Existing | To allow you choosing one or more existing elements from the model and add a new rows to the data table. |
| Delete from Table | To remove the selected element and row from the data table. |
| Delete | To remove the selected element and row from the data table and the model. |
| Default Owner | To define Constrains and Measurement Sets default ownership. |
| Move Up | To move the selected row up. |
| Move Down | To move the selected row down. |
| Show Columns | To open the shortcut menu with a list of the available columns for the data table.You will find that currently displayed columns are selected.Click an unselected column to display it.Click a selected column to hide it. |
| Report | To create a corresponding spreadsheet report by passing all the elements form the data table directly to the report. The Report button is available for CV-5, OV-3 (DoDAF, MODAF, and NAF), OV-6a (DoDAF, MODAF, and NAF), SV-6, SV-7, SV-10a, SOV-4a, SvcV-6, SvcV-7, and SvcV-10a, StV-5 tables. You can generate either an OpenDocument (.ods) or Microsoft Excel 2007 (.xslx) spreadsheet report. |
| Export | To export an active table either to a plain text file (.cvs) or to a Hypertext Markup Language (.html) format. |
| Show Full Types | To show or hide the full path (qualified name) of each element used in the data table. |

Tables are fully synchronized with model which means that modification to the table such as deleting or creating will affect model elements except for a few cases that are described within the product specification sections. 
 
 Some tables, such as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P'], allow rows filtering. 
 
 To filter row

1. Create an OV-3 or SV-6 table.
2. On the table toolbar, click the Show Rows button.
3. Deselect row types that you want to hide or select row types that you want to show in the table.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

export an active table either to a plain text file (.cvs) or to a Hypertext Markup Language (.html) format.</td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Show</strong> <strong>Full Types</strong></p></td><td>To show or hide the full path (qualified name) of each element used in the data table.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>Tables are fully synchronized with model which means that modification to the table such as Some tables, such as <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-6]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, allow rows filtering. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To filter row</p><hr /><ol><li>Create an OV-3 or SV-6 table.</li><li>On the table toolbar, click the <strong>Show </strong><strong>Rows</strong> button.</li><li>Deselect row types that you want to hide or select row types that you want to show in the table.</li></ol><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="7729c8c8-ef46-46c0-9341-3127686058fe" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575249 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01559: Managing Version of Configurations in SvcV-8 diagram

- page_id: `249575249`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575249/Managing+Version+of+Configurations+in+SvcV-8+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T11:20:24.481+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description
- labels: ['add-version-of-configuration-in-svcv-8', 'delete-version-of-configuration-in-svcv-8', 'manage-version-of-configurations-in-svcv-8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a Version of Configuration

1. On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Version of Configuration . An empty line appears in the diagram pane.
2. Type its name.

To delete a Version of Configuration

1. Select a Version of Configuration.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Delete button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To add a Version of Configuration<span> </span></p><hr /><ol><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt;<strong> Version of Configuration</strong>. An empty line appears in the diagram pane.</li><li>Type its name.</li></ol><p>To delete a Version of Configuration</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Version of Configuration.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Delete </strong>button.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: Asserts that a StandardOperationalActivity is in some way part of a capability.

DoDAF: MapsToCapability (DoDAF::ActivityPartOfCapability) is a disposition to manifest an Activity. An Activity to be performed to achieve a desired effect under specified [performance] standards and conditions through combinations of ways and means.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Dependency

##### Related DoDAF views

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575936 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01567: Measurements dialog

- page_id: `249575936`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575936/Measurements+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-13T15:13:54.899+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Dialogs
- labels: ['measurements-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Measurements** dialog is designed to display measurements available to be added as columns to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P'], or[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P'] tables.

The **Measurements** dialog consists of two tabs:

- Operational Exchange (or Resource Interaction in the SV-6 and SvcV-6 tables).
- Operational Exchange Item (or Resource Interaction Item in the SV-6 and SvcV-6 tables).

The**Operational Exchange** (or **Resource Interaction** in the SV-6 and SvcV-6 tables) tab displays measurements grouped by the Measurement Sets of the Operational Exchanges (or Resource Interactions in the SV-6 and SvcV-6 tables). The **Operational Exchange Item** (or**Resource Interaction Item** in the SV-6 and SvcV-6 tables) tab displays measurements grouped by the Measurement Sets of Operational Exchange Items (or Resource Interaction Items in the SV-6 and SvcV-6 tables). The measurements displayed in the **Measurements** dialog are collected from the active table.

The purpose of the Measurement Set is grouping the particular measurements. In order to show or hide measurements of a particular Measurement Set, you can expand or collapse the Measurement Set category. For example, Exchange Properties is the grouping Measurement Set of the measurements Accountability, Interoperability Level Achievable, and so forth.

When you select the measurement and click the **OK** button, the selected measurement is added as a new column into the table.

The selected measurement description is displayed in the Description area. If you want to see the description of your custom measurement, you should have specified the documentation when creating a measurement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Measurements** dialog

- On the active table toolbar, click Show Columns button and select Measurements . The Measurements dialog opens.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying Custom Measurements' space='UPDM2P']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <strong>Measurements</strong> dialog is designed to display measurements available to be added as columns to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-6]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, or<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ SvcV-6]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> tables.</p><p>The <strong>Measurements</strong> dialog consists of two tabs:</p><ul><li><strong>Operational Exchange</strong> (or <strong>Resource Interaction</strong> in the SV-6 and SvcV-6 tables).</li><li><strong>Operational Exchange Item</strong> (or <strong>Resource Interaction Item</strong> in the SV-6 and SvcV-6 tables).</li></ul><p>The<strong> Operational Exchange</strong> (or <strong>Resource Interaction</strong> in the SV-6 and SvcV-6 tables) tab displays measurements grouped by the Measurement Sets of the Operational Exchanges (or Resource Interactions in the SV-6 and SvcV-6 tables). The <strong>Operational Exchange Item</strong> (or<strong> Resource Interaction Item</strong> in the SV-6 and SvcV-6 tables) tab displays measurements grouped by the Measurement Sets of Operational Exchange Items (or Resource Interaction Items in the SV-6 and SvcV-6 tables). The measurements displayed in the <strong>Measurements</strong> dialog are collected from the active table.</p><p>The purpose of the Measurement Set is grouping the particular measurements. In order to show or hide measurements of a particular Measurement Set, you can expand or collapse the Measurement Set category. For example, Exchange Properties is the grouping Measurement Set of the measurements Accountability, Interoperability Level Achievable, and so forth.</p><p>When you select the measurement and click the <strong>OK</strong> button, the selected measurement is added as a new column into the table.</p><p>The selected measurement description is displayed in the Description area. If you want to see the description of your custom measurement, you should have specified the documentation when creating a measurement.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Measurements dialog" ac:title="Measurements dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurements_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Measurements dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p><p>To open the <strong>Measurements</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>On the active table toolbar, click <strong>Show Columns</strong> button and select<strong> Measurements</strong>.<br />The <strong>Measurements</strong> dialog opens.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="020d71b1-3dd7-4c96-9c20-71993c04d3da" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Displaying Custom Measurements" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575326 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01573: MODAF All Views Viewpoint

- page_id: `249575326`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575326/MODAF+All+Views+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:14.178+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Elements that are part of the All View. The All-Views (AVs) provide an overarching description of the architecture, its scope, ownership, timeframe and all of the other meta data that is required in order to effectively search and query architectural models. They also provide a place to record any findings arising from the architecting process. The AVs include a dictionary of the terms used in the construction of the architecture – which helps others fully understand its meaning at a later date. Since the AVs provide critical information for the future access and exploitation of an architectural model their population is essential whenever an architecture is created or modified. The AVs provide a critical input into the processes that provide architectural governance.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Elements that are part of the All View. The All-Views (AVs) provide an overarching description of the architecture, its scope, ownership, timeframe and all of the other meta data that is required in order to effectively search and query architectural models. They also provide a place to record any findings arising from the architecting process. The AVs include a dictionary of the terms used in the construction of the architecture – which helps others fully understand its meaning at a later date. Since the AVs provide critical information for the future access and exploitation of an architectural model their population is essential whenever an architecture is created or modified. The AVs provide a critical input into the processes that provide architectural governance.</p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="0cab72b5-e739-4d1d-a9cb-b3bc1f4acb6c" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575354 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01574: MODAF Operational Viewpoint

- page_id: `249575354`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575354/MODAF+Operational+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:14.604+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Viewpoint is about real-world activities, the people and machinery that perform them, and the means by which they are performed. The Operational Viewpoint is divided into nine views intended to answer the “who”, “what”, “when”, “where”, “why”, and “how” of a mission.

The Operational Views are common to MODAF and DoDAF; OV-1b, OV-1c, and OV-2 however, have been customized to provide for MOD requirements. The Operational Views describe the tasks and activities, operational elements, and information exchanges required to conduct operations. In MODAF thinking, the OV Views are considered to illustrate the Logical Architecture of the enterprise.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Operational Viewpoint is about real-world activities, the people and machinery that perform them, and the means by which they are performed. The Operational Viewpoint is divided into nine views intended to answer the “who”, “what”, “when”, “where”, “why”, and “how” of a mission.</p><p>The Operational Views are common to MODAF and DoDAF; OV-1b, OV-1c, and OV-2 however, have been customized to provide for MOD requirements. The Operational Views describe the tasks and activities, operational elements, and information exchanges required to conduct operations. In MODAF thinking, the OV Views are considered to illustrate the Logical Architecture of the enterprise.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51021151-7b24-4f28-ab31-f3d730d0b90f"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jrpPuZ8Vf74" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br />The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="bd3046ba-6e5c-4b3c-ae1c-08f58ae2ec47" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575376 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01575: MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint

- page_id: `249575376`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575376/MODAF+Service+Oriented+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:14.881+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Services View (SOV) shows the elements that are part of SOV-1 through SOV-5.

The Service-Orientated View is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the Operational View. A service is described as a unit of work through which a particular Resource provides a useful result to a consuming Resource.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Services View (SOV) shows the elements that are part of SOV-1 through SOV-5.</p><p>The Service-Orientated View is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the Operational View. A service is described as a unit of work through which a particular Resource provides a useful result to a consuming Resource.</p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="d78799f7-2af7-4a55-b6b2-d216aa859572" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575332 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01576: MODAF Strategic Viewpoint

- page_id: `249575332`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575332/MODAF+Strategic+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:14.368+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Strategic View (StV) shows the elements that are part of StV-1 through StV-6. The Strategic Elements are used in the Strategic View that provides an overall Enterprise Architecture assessment of the Capabilities and their relationships facilitating Capability Management (e.g. capability introduction, integration, re-alignment and removal). While an Enterprise will have a number of UPDM Architecture Descriptions that have the Operational, System, Technical Standards, and All Views, only one Strategic View will exist across a number of Architecture Descriptions.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Strategic View (StV) shows the elements that are part of StV-1 through StV-6. The Strategic Elements are used in the Strategic View that provides an overall Enterprise Architecture assessment of the Capabilities and their relationships facilitating Capability Management (e.g. capability introduction, integration, re-alignment and removal). While an Enterprise will have a number of UPDM Architecture Descriptions that have the Operational, System, Technical Standards, and All Views, only one Strategic View will exist across a number of Architecture  Descriptions.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fa507d40-6514-46a8-856e-fb8b6a3a2dd0"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xD91EYVU-bM" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1e62cfd-c6c8-4fe1-98e3-466160bb9013"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LB1Ure3ravQ" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="71f190eb-5b5e-47a3-b706-3d96d45eeff2" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575392 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01577: MODAF Systems Viewpoint

- page_id: `249575392`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575392/MODAF+Systems+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.114+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The System View (SV) shows the elements that are part of SV-1 through SV-12. Models in the System Viewpoint represent alternate realizations in terms of equipment capability of the operational capabilities expressed through models in the Operational Viewpoint and in the User Requirements. The System Viewpoint primarily addresses the specification of the system capability needed (rather than implementation details). Significant changes originally made in MODAF improved the ability for modelers to represent configuration of capability that include people as well as systems and platforms.

The views of this view are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The System View (SV) shows the elements that are part of SV-1 through SV-12. Models in the System Viewpoint represent alternate realizations in terms of equipment capability of the operational capabilities expressed through models in the Operational Viewpoint and in the User Requirements. The System Viewpoint primarily addresses the specification of the system capability needed (rather than implementation details). Significant changes originally made in MODAF improved the ability for modelers to represent configuration of capability that include people as well as systems and platforms.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f36730a-3425-4657-b9c9-b69e909746b6"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qvL33ckmJJY" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>The views of this view are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="27663c43-db4d-4fc0-bf8e-02615a02e2aa" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575429 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01578: MODAF Technical Standards Viewpoint

- page_id: `249575429`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575429/MODAF+Technical+Standards+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.766+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Technical View (TV) shows the elements that are part of TV-1 through TV-2.

The Technical View is a set of views delineating standards, rules, notations, and conventions that apply to the implementation of the system architecture. When the standards profile is tied to the system elements to which they apply, TV-1 serves as the bridge between the SV and TV. SV-9 forecasts relate to the TV-1 in that a timed technology forecast may contribute to the decision to retire or phase out the use of a certain standard in connection with a system element. Similarly, SV-9 forecasts relate to TV-2 standards forecasts in that a certain standard may be adopted depending on a certain technology becoming available (e.g., the availability of Java Script may influence the decision to adopt a new HTML standard).

MODAF extends the core DoDAF Technical Standards Views to include non-technical standards and policies applicable to the architecture such as operational doctrine, industry process standards, etc. Additionally, the TV-1 may also document policies and standards applicable to the operational or business context. MODAF also distinguishes between ‘applicability’ and ‘conformance’ with regard to architectural elements. If a standard is applicable to a given architecture, that architecture need not be fully conformant with the standard. The degree of conformance to a given standard may be judged on a risk basis at an approval point. An association between a Standard and an architectural element is not to be interpreted as stating the level of compliance of the element is fully compliant with that Standard. Additional evidences would need to be given (outside MODAF) to confirm the level of compliance. Finally, MODAF adds the explicit requirement that any Standards cited in TV-1 View must, where appropriate, be in accordance with the trend towards open architectures – i.e. standards which encourage stove-piped systems are expressly prohibited.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Technical View (TV) shows the elements that are part of TV-1 through TV-2. </p><p>The Technical View is a set of views delineating standards, rules, notations, and conventions that apply to the implementation of the system architecture. When the standards profile is tied to the system elements to which they apply, TV-1 serves as the bridge between the SV and TV. SV-9 forecasts relate to the TV-1 in that a timed technology forecast may contribute to the decision to retire or phase out the use of a certain standard in connection with a system element. Similarly, SV-9 forecasts relate to TV-2 standards forecasts in that a certain standard may be adopted depending on a certain technology becoming available (e.g., the availability of Java Script may influence the decision to adopt a new HTML standard). </p><p>MODAF extends the core DoDAF Technical Standards Views to include non-technical standards and policies applicable to the architecture such as operational doctrine, industry process standards, etc. Additionally, the TV-1 may also document policies and standards applicable to the operational or business context. MODAF also distinguishes between ‘applicability’ and ‘conformance’ with regard to architectural elements. If a standard is applicable to a given architecture, that architecture need not be fully conformant with the standard. The degree of conformance to a given standard may be judged on a risk basis at an approval point. An association between a Standard and an architectural element is not to be interpreted as stating the level of compliance of the element is fully compliant with that Standard. Additional evidences would need to be given (outside MODAF) to confirm the level of compliance. Finally, MODAF adds the explicit requirement that any Standards cited in TV-1 View must, where appropriate, be in accordance with the trend towards open architectures – i.e. standards which encourage stove-piped systems are expressly prohibited.</p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="77a7ee4f-cf13-45cc-8593-ecdc77324b42" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575350 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01579: MODAF. AcV-1 Acquisition Clusters

- page_id: `249575350`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575350/MODAF.+AcV-1+Acquisition+Clusters
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:14.561+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Acquisition Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Acquisition Clusters (AcV-1) View describes how acquisition projects are grouped in organisational terms as a coherent portfolio of acquisition programmes.

The AcV-1 View provides a way of describing the organizational relationships between multiple acquisition projects, each of which is responsible for delivering individual systems or capabilities. By definition, this View covers acquisition programmes consisting of multiple projects and will generally not be developed by those building Architectures for an individual project. In essence, the AcV-1 is an organizational breakdown consisting of actual organizations (see OV-4). The view is strongly linked with the StV-4 which shows capability clusters and dependencies.

The AcV-1 View is hierarchical in nature. Higher level groupings of projects (or, rather the organisations that own these projects) form acquisition clusters.

The intent of an AcV-1 View Product is to show:

- All of the acquisition projects delivering systems or system of systems (SoS) within the acquisition programmes under consideration.
- Other systems and SoS which may have a bearing on the Architecture.
- How the systems will be best integrated into acquisition clusters.
- The nesting of acquisition clusters to form a hierarchy.

**Implementation**

AcV-1 can be represented using:

- An AcV-1 Acquisition Clusters diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- An AcV-1 Responsibility Matrix which is an editable Dependency Matrix.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### AcV-1 Responsibility Matrix

**Related views**

In essence, the AcV-1 is an organizational breakdown consisting of actual organizations (see OV-4). The view is strongly linked with the StV-4 which shows capability clusters and dependencies.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Actual Organization
- Actual Post
- Actual Project
- Organizational Project Relationship
- Project Ownership
- Project Sequence
- Project Type
- Project Milestone
- Project Milestone Role
- Actual Project Milestone Role
- Status Indicators

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating AcV-1 diagram
- Building AcV-1 matrix

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

By definition, this View covers acquisition programmes consisting of multiple projects and will generally not be developed by those building Architectures for an individual project. In essence, the AcV-1 is an organizational breakdown consisting of actual organizations (see OV-4). The view is strongly linked with the StV-4 which shows capability clusters and dependencies.</p><p>The AcV-1 View is hierarchical in nature. Higher level groupings of projects (or, rather the organisations that own these projects) form acquisition clusters.</p><p>The intent of an AcV-1 View Product is to show:</p><ul><li>All of the acquisition projects delivering systems or system of systems (SoS) within the acquisition programmes under consideration.</li><li>Other systems and SoS which may have a bearing on the Architecture.</li><li>How the systems will be best integrated into acquisition clusters.</li><li>The nesting of acquisition clusters to form a hierarchy.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>AcV-1 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>An AcV-1 Acquisition Clusters diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>An AcV-1 Responsibility Matrix which is an editable Dependency Matrix.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Acv_1.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="MODAF. AcV-1 Acquisition Clusters" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>AcV-1 Responsibility Matrix</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>In essence, the AcV-1 is an organizational breakdown consisting of actual organizations (see OV-4). <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575352 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01580: MODAF. AcV-2 Programme Timelines

- page_id: `249575352`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575352/MODAF.+AcV-2+Programme+Timelines
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:14.582+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Acquisition Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The AcV-2 view provides a timeline perspective on programmes.

The AcV-2 View is intended primarily to support the acquisition and fielding processes including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of all the DLODs to achieve a successfully integrated military capability.

For capability-based procurement, these work streams might conveniently be equated with Defence Lines of Development (DLODs).

**Implementation**

AcV-2 can be represented using an AcV-2 diagram which is realized as a Gantt Chart.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Acv-2 Programme Timelines

**Related views**

The use of AcV-2 should support the management of capability delivery and be aligned with the capability phasing view (StV-3), if one exists.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Actual Project
- Project Status
- Project Sequence
- Project Theme
- Actual Project Milestone
- Increment Milestone
- Out Of Service Milestone
- Milestone Sequence
- Project Milestone

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating AcV-2 diagram

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Project Status Application Wizard

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575327 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01581: MODAF. AV-1 Overview and Summary Information

- page_id: `249575327`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575327/MODAF.+AV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:14.186+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF All Views Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The overview and summary information contained within the AV-1 product provides an executive-level summary information in a consistent form that allows for quick reference and comparison between architectural descriptions. AV-1 includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture. In an enterprise repository environment, individual architectures are mapped against enterprise phases to provide context between the architectures. AV-1 is usually a structured text product. Organizations may create a template for the AV-1 that can then be used to create a consistent set of information across different architecture-based projects.

**Implementation**

AV-1 can be represented:

- Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial AV-1 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.
- Using a AV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fragment of AV-1 Overview and Summary Information report

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Architectural Description
- Defines Architecture
- Architecture Metadata

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing reports' space='UPDM2P']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575329 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01582: MODAF. AV-2 Integrated Dictionary

- page_id: `249575329`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575329/MODAF.+AV-2+Integrated+Dictionary
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:14.337+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF All Views Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An AV-2 presents all the Elements used in an architecture as a standalone structure. An AV-2 presents all the Elements as a specialization hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element. An AV-2 shows elements from the Ontology that have been used in the architecture and new elements that have been introduced by the architecture. Architectures often introduce new terms – usually because the architecture is covering new technology or business processes. The purpose of the AV-2 is to provide a local extension of the Ontology to explain the terms and abbreviations used in building the architecture.

**Implementation**

AV-2 can be represented:

- Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data.
- An AV-2 table. If you have AV-2 diagrams from previous versions, they will be migrated into the AV-1 diagram when loading the project with UPDM 17.0.2.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### AV-2 Integrated Dictionary

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fragment of AV-2 Integrated Dictionary report

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Definition
- Alias
- Same As

#### PANEL: Related procedure

Related procedure

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing reports' space='UPDM2P']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575355 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01583: MODAF. OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic

- page_id: `249575355`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575355/MODAF.+OV-1+High-Level+Operational+Concept+Graphic
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:14.613+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The purpose of High-level Operational Concept Graphic is to provide a high-level graphical and textual description of operational concept (high level organizations, missions, geographic configuration, connectivity, etc) of what the architecture is supposed to do, and how it is supposed to do it. The OV-1, along with the corresponding AV-1 product is intended to serve as an executive summary of the architecture.

**Implementation**

OV-1 can be represented using:

- A link to an external document.
- An OV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- An OV-1 Free Form diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure Diagram.

**Sample** 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- High-Level Operational Concept
- Concept Role
- Arbitrary Relationship

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating OV-1 diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575357 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01584: MODAF. OV-2 Operational Node Internal Relationship Description

- page_id: `249575357`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575357/MODAF.+OV-2+Operational+Node+Internal+Relationship+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:14.635+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Implementation**

OV-2 can be represented using:

- An OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- An OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### OV-2 Operational Node Internal Relationship Description

**Related views**

An OV-2 is highly related with an OV-5. Operational Nodes shown in the OV-2 are the performers of the Operational Activities modeled in the OV-5. OV-2 focuses on the Operational Nodes, with the activities being a secondary adornment. The OV-5, on the other hand, places first-order attention on operational activities and only second-order attention on Nodes, which can be shown as annotations or swim-lanes on the activities.

Information flows can be modeled either in the OV-2 or OV-5. In both cases they are highly associated and in general should be reused between these views.

The OV-2 displays the Capabilities required by Nodes from StV-2. That is an association between two abstraction levels of user requirements where the OV is more specific than the StV and a Node is more specific concept than a Capability.

The other important mapping is between OV-2 and SV-1. The specification Node and implementation Resource are subjects to map here. One OV-2 product can have several implementations in the SV-1.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Known Resource
- Needline
- Node Port
- Node Role
- Operational Exchange
- Service Channel
- Service
- Property
- Request

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating OV-2 diagram
- Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

In both cases they are highly associated and in general should be reused between these views.</p><p>The OV-2 displays the Capabilities required by Nodes from StV-2. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575359 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01585: MODAF. OV-2 Operational Node Relationship Description

- page_id: `249575359`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575359/MODAF.+OV-2+Operational+Node+Relationship+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:14.659+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Operational Node Connectivity Description is intended to track the need to exchange information from specific operational nodes (that play a key role in the architecture) to others. An OV-2 does not depict the connectivity between the nodes. MODAF modifies the OV-2 in two ways. First it recommends that an OV-2 diagram (now OV2a) shows the platforms or geographic locations at which operational nodes are deployed. Secondly it provides additional information (OV-2b) about each needline in the form of a requirements specification. There are now four types of needlines identified as follows:

1. InformationExchange
2. EnergyFlow
3. MaterielFlow
4. MovementOfPeople

In addition, MODAF permits service-oriented architectures. Instead of needlines between nodes, it is possible simply to show which services the nodes provide and consume. Finally, MODAF again permits known resources to be shown in an OV-2. However, this must be clearly shown as a KnownResource in an OV-2 model. LogicalArchitecture, which is the container class for all the nodes and KnownResources, is introduced.

**Implementation**

OV-2 can be represented using:

- An OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- An OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### OV-2 Operational Node Relationship Description

**Related views**

An OV-2 is highly related with an OV-5. Operational Nodes shown in the OV-2 are the performers of the Operational Activities modeled in the OV-5. OV-2 focuses on the Operational Nodes, with the activities being a secondary adornment. The OV-5, on the other hand, places first-order attention on operational activities and only second-order attention on Nodes, which can be shown as annotations or swim-lanes on the activities.

Information flows can be modeled either in the OV-2 or OV-5. In both cases they are highly associated and in general should be reused between these views.

The OV-2 displays the Capabilities required by Nodes from StV-2. That is an association between two abstraction levels of user requirements where the OV is more specific than the StV and a Node is more specific concept than a Capability.

The other important mapping is between OV-2 and SV-1. The specification Node and implementation Resource are subjects to map here. One OV-2 product can have several implementations in the SV-1.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Node
- Logical Architecture
- Operational Exchange
- Exchange Element
- Node Port
- Node Role
- Needline
- Capability
- Exhibits
- Actual Location
- Location Type
- Energy
- Materiel
- Is Capable Of Performing
- Operational Activity
- Known Resource
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Problem Domain
- Security Domain

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating OV-2 diagram
- Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 diagram

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Operational Exchange Creation Wizard
- Operational Exchange Manager Dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575361 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01586: MODAF. OV-3 Operational Information Exchange Matrix

- page_id: `249575361`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575361/MODAF.+OV-3+Operational+Information+Exchange+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:14.683+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Information exchanges express the relationship across the three basic architecture data elements of an OV (operational activities, operational nodes, and information flow) with a focus on the specific aspects of the information flow and the information content.

The Information Exchanges of the OV-3 should remain at a high level of aggregation to represent actual information workflow products that are used at the operational nodes shown in the OV-2 (and not their subordinate operational nodes).

**Implementation**

OV-3 can be represented using:

- An OV-3 table.
- An OV-3 role-based table. A role-based table represents exchanges between Node Roles (node usages).
- An OV-3 spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### OV-3 Operational Information Exchange Matrix

**Related views**

An OV-3 is initially constructed from the information contained in the Operational Node Connectivity Description (OV-2).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Node
- Operational Exchange
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel
- Resource Artifact
- Post
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Operational Activity

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating OV-3 table
- Displaying Custom Measurements

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575363 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01587: MODAF. OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart

- page_id: `249575363`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575363/MODAF.+OV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:14.707+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Organizational Relationships Chart illustrates the command structure or relationships (as opposed to relationships with respect to a business process flow) among human roles, organizations, or organization types that are the key players in architecture. MODAF divides The OV-4 in two views: an OV-4 Typical and an OV-4 Actual. The former is exactly as the DoDAF OV-4, while the latter is a special form of the SV-1; where the resources are restricted to being organizational.

**Implementation**

OV-4 can be represented using:

- An OV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart

**Related views**

The organizations that are modeled using an OV-4 in the Operational Viewpoint may also appear in other views, for example, SV-1 (organizational constituents of a capability configuration), AcV-1 (actual organizations that own projects), and SV-5 (organizational resources performs functions).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Organization
- Post
- Person
- Command
- Competence
- Provides Competence
- Requires Competence
- Actual Organization
- Actual Post
- Actual Person
- Fills Post
- Actual Organization Relationship
- Actual Organization Role
- Operational Activity
- Owns Process

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating OV-4 diagram
- Instantiating Structures

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575365 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01588: MODAF. OV-5 Operational Activity Model

- page_id: `249575365`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575365/MODAF.+OV-5+Operational+Activity+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:14.735+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Operational Activity Model describes the operations that are normally conducted in the course of achieving a mission or a business goal, from a net-centric perspective. It describes capabilities, operational activities (or tasks), input and output (I/O) flows between activities, and I/O flows to/from activities that are outside the scope of the architecture. It is imperative that the levels-of-detail between the OV-2, OV-3, and OV-5 remain cohesive. For example, if one diagram of OV-2 operational nodes is developed that shows aggregated organizations only, then it is imperative that the corresponding OV-5 product be developed to show only those operational activities that are meaningful with respect to these operational nodes. Similarly, the information exchanges of OV-3 should remain at a high level of aggregation to represent actual information workflow products that are used at the operational nodes depicted in OV-2 (and not their subordinate operational nodes). The net-centric OV-5 may be used in the following ways:

- Delineate lines of dependency on external activities when coupled with an OV-2.
- Highlight information flows to depict the status of the information's refinement (raw, preprocessed, fused, etc.).
- Provide the critical foundation for depicting Task, Post, Process, and Use (TPPU) activity sequencing and timing in the OV-6a, OV-6b, and OV-6c.
- Identify critical mission threads and operational information exchanges by annotating which activities are critical, i.e., identify the activities in the model that are critical.

**Implementation**

OV-5 can be represented using:

- An OV-5 diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram.
- An OV-5 diagram for Operational Activity flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Activity diagram.
- A SysML Block diagram.
- A SysML Activity diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### OV-5 Operational Activity hierarchy Model

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fragment of OV-5 Operational Activity Model

**Related views**

The OV-5 and OV-2 are, to a degree, complements of each other. An OV-5 focuses on the operational activities whereas OV-2 focuses on the operational nodes. Due to the relationship between nodes and operational activities, these types of view should normally be developed together.

To maintain this independence from implementation, the logical Nodes in an OV-2 are used to represent the structure which carries out the Operational Activities. Operational Activities are realized as Functions (SV-4) which are the “how” to the Operational Activities’ “what”, for example, they are specified in terms of the resources that carry them out.

The activities described in an OV-5 may be Standard Operational Activities which are defined in the StV-6 (which also maps the activities to corresponding capabilities).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Operational Activity
- Operational Parameter
- Standard Operational Activity
- Node
- Is Capable Of Performing
- Operational Activity Action
- Operational Activity Edge
- Operational Exchange
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating OV-5 Operational Activity Model diagram
- Creating OV-5 Operational Activity Flow Model diagram
- Displaying possible Operational Exchanges on the selected Operational Activity Edge

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Operational Exchange Creation Wizard
- Operational Exchange Manager Dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It describes capabilities, operational activities (or tasks), input and output (I/O) flows between activities, and I/O flows to/from activities that are outside the scope of the architecture. For example, if one diagram of OV-2 operational nodes is developed that shows aggregated organizations only, then it is imperative that the corresponding OV-5 product be developed to show only those operational activities that are meaningful with respect to these operational nodes. The net-centric OV-5 may be used in the following ways:</p><ul><li>Delineate lines of dependency on external activities when coupled with an OV-2.</li><li>Highlight information flows to depict the status of the information's refinement (raw, preprocessed, fused, etc.).</li><li>Provide the critical foundation for depicting Task, Post, Process, and Use (TPPU) activity sequencing and timing in the OV-6a, OV-6b, and OV-6c.</li><li>Identify critical mission threads and operational information exchanges by annotating which activities are critical, i.e., identify the activities in the model that are critical.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>OV-5 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>An OV-5 diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies.
