<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575402 space=UPDM2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 01616: MODAF. SV-4 Functionality Description

- page_id: `249575402`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575402/MODAF.+SV-4+Functionality+Description
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:59:36.133+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Functionality Descriptions (SV-4) address human and system functionality. The primary purposes of SV-4 are 
to develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by 
each resource, ensure that the functional connectivity is complete, and ensure that the functional decomposition 
reaches an appropriate level of detail. The Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the 
allocation of functions to resources and flow of data between functions. The SV-4 is the systems view counterpart.

**Implementation**

SV-4 can be represented using:

- A SV-4 diagram for Function hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram.
- An SV-4 diagram for Function flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Activity diagram.
- A SysML Block diagram.
- A SysML Activity diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fragment of SV-4 Functionality Description Flow

You can also create SV-4 Business Process Diagram (BPD). In order to do that, use the Cameo Business Modeler plugin. For more information on how to model the business process diagram, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='BPMN Process Diagram' space='MT'].

**Related views**

An SV-4 is the behavioral counterpart to the SV-1 (in the same way that OV-5 is the behavioral counterpart to OV2).

The functions are likely to be related to Operational Activities captured in an OV-5. Although there is a correlation between the Operational Activity Model (OV-5) and the functional hierarchy of SV-4, it need not be a one-to-one mapping, hence, the need for a Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (SV-5), which provides that mapping.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Function
- Operational Activity
- Standard Operational Activity
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Is Capable Of Performing
- Function Action
- Function Edge
- Function Parameter
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating SV-4 Functionality Description diagram
- Creating SV-4 Functionality Description Flow diagram

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Resource Interaction Creation Wizard
- Resource Interaction Manager Dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Functionality Descriptions (SV-4) address human and system functionality. The primary purposes of SV-4 are<br />to develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by<br />each resource, ensure that the functional connectivity is complete, and ensure that the functional decomposition<br />reaches an appropriate level of detail. The Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the<br />allocation of functions to resources and flow of data between functions. The SV-4 is the systems view counterpart.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-4 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A SV-4 diagram for Function hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>An SV-4 diagram for Function flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram.</li><li>A UML Class diagram.</li><li>A UML Activity diagram.</li><li>A SysML Block diagram.</li><li>A SysML Activity diagram.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sv_4.jpg" /></ac:image></p><h6>Fragment of SV-4 Functionality Description Flow</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82b19689-89ef-4e4e-b87f-3669ed85daee"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also create SV-4 Business Process Diagram (BPD). In order to do that, use the Cameo Business Modeler plugin. For more information on how to model the business process diagram, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="BPMN Process Diagram" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>An SV-4 is the behavioral counterpart to the SV-1 (in the same way that OV-5 is the behavioral counterpart to OV2).</p><p>The functions are likely to be related to Operational Activities captured in an OV-5. Although there is a correlation between the Operational Activity Model (OV-5) and the functional hierarchy of SV-4, it need not be a one-to-one mapping, hence, the need for a Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (SV-5), which provides that mapping.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8d689af-ccb0-46f1-b0e4-e0ef8f59eba7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Function</li><li>Operational Activity</li><li>Standard Operational Activity</li><li>Resource Artifact</li><li>Software</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Organization</li><li>Post</li><li>Is Capable Of Performing</li><li>Function Action</li><li>Function Edge</li><li>Function Parameter</li><li>Resource Interaction</li><li>Exchange Element</li><li>Energy</li><li>Materiel</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c056737-5485-461e-96a1-3c59320ca315"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating SV-4 Functionality Description diagram</li><li>Creating SV-4 Functionality Description Flow diagram</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc1d56c6-6da4-4562-bcb2-024645fab5db"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related references</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Interaction Creation Wizard</li><li>Resource Interaction Manager Dialog</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575404 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01617: MODAF. SV-5 Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `249575404`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575404/MODAF.+SV-5+Function+to+Operational+Activity+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.309+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (SV-5) addresses the linkage between functions described in an SV-4 and Operational Activities specified in an OV-5. The SV-5 View depicts the mapping of functions (and, optionally, the functional resources that provide them) to operational activities and thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a system or solution.

**Implementation**

SV-5 can be represented using a SV-5 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Functions will be used as the row elements and the Operational Activities will be used as the column elements.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-5 Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

**Related views**

An SV-5 addresses the linkage between the functions described in an SV-4 and the Operational Activities specified in an OV-5.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Function
- Operational Activity
- Implements

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Building SV-5 matrix

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The SV-5 View depicts the mapping of functions (and, optionally, the functional resources that provide them) to operational activities and thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a system or solution.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-5 can be represented using a SV-5 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575406 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01618: MODAF. SV-6 Systems Data Exchange Matrix

- page_id: `249575406`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575406/MODAF.+SV-6+Systems+Data+Exchange+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.349+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Systems Data Exchange Matrix specifies the characteristics of the system data exchanged between systems. The focus is on data crossing the system boundary. An SV-6 focuses on the specific aspects of the system data flow and the system data content in a tabular format.

**Implementation**

SV-6 can be represented using:

- A SV-6 table.
- An SV-6 role-based table. A role-based table represents exchanges between Resource Roles(resource usages).
- A SV-6 spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Systems Data Exchange Matrix

**Related views**

SV-6 is the physical equivalent of the logical OV-3 table and provides detailed information on the system connections which implement the information exchanges specified in an OV-3.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Exchange Element
- Resource Interaction
- Materiel
- Energy

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Adding Existing Resource Interaction to SV-6 table

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Systems Data Exchange Matrix specifies the characteristics of the system data exchanged between systems. The focus is on data crossing the system boundary. An SV-6 focuses on the specific aspects of the system data flow and the system data content in a tabular format.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-6 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A SV-6 table.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">An SV-6 role-based table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b11eb514-520f-4cf6-84e4-90140cad3034"><ac:rich-text-body>A role-based table represents exchanges between Resource Roles(resource usages).</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>A SV-6 spreadsheet report.</li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sv_6.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="MODAF. SV-6 Systems Data Exchange Matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Systems Data Exchange Matrix</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>SV-6 is the physical equivalent of the logical OV-3 table and provides detailed information on the system connections which implement the information exchanges specified in an OV-3.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51e25b28-7ada-4097-b827-be1db714363f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Artifact</li><li>Software</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Organization</li><li>Post</li><li>Exchange Element</li><li>Resource Interaction</li><li>Materiel</li><li>Energy</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="218fe2ab-053d-4825-815d-91a1382eb8ab"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Adding Existing Resource Interaction to SV-6 table</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575408 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01619: MODAF. SV-7 Resource Performance Parameters Matrix

- page_id: `249575408`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575408/MODAF.+SV-7+Resource+Performance+Parameters+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.392+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-7 is the Resource Performance Parameters Matrix and depicts the performance characteristics of a Functional Resource (system, role, or capability configuration). The Resource Performance Parameters Matrix expands on the information presented in an SV-1 by depicting the characteristics of the Functional Resources shown in the SV-1. The Resource Performance Parameters Matrix View specifies qualitative and quantitative characteristics of functional resources and the performance parameters of each resource. The performance parameters are selected by the architect and end user community.

**Implementation**

SV-7 can be represented using:

- A SV-7 typical parameters table.
- A SV-7 actual parameters table.
- A SV-7 actual parameters spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-7 Typical table

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-7 Actual table

**Related views**

| Icon | View | Relationship |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification | The Resource Performance Parameters Matrix expands on the information presented in an SV-1 by depicting the characteristics of the resources shown in the SV-1. |
|  | SV-2 Resource Communications Description | SV-7 expands on the information presented in an SV-2 by depicting the characteristics of the Resources shown in the SV-2. |

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Artifact
- Software
- System
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Measurement Set
- Actual Property Set
- Measurement
- Actual Measurement

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating SV-7 Typical table
- Creating SV-7 Actual table
- Generating SV-7 Actual table from SV-7 Typical table
- Generating reports

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575413 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01620: MODAF. SV-8 Capability Configuration Management

- page_id: `249575413`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575413/MODAF.+SV-8+Capability+Configuration+Management
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.479+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Capability Configuration Management view presents a whole lifecycle view of a resource, describing how its configuration changes over time. The SV-8 provides an overview of how a capability configuration structure changes over time. It shows the structure of several capability configurations mapped against a timeline.

**Implementation**

SV-8 can be represented using a SV-8 diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-8 Capability Configuration Management

**Related views**

| Icon | View | Relationship |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |
|  |  | The changes depicted in the SV-8 View are derived from the project milestones that are shown using an AcV-2. When the AcV-2 is used to model project timelines for capability acquisition projects, there is likely to be a close relationship between these two Views. |

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Whole Life Configuration
- Version Of Configuration
- Resource Role
- Resource Interface

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575417 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01621: MODAF. SV-9 Technology & Skills Forecast

- page_id: `249575417`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575417/MODAF.+SV-9+Technology+Skills+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.548+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Technology & Skills Forecast defines the underlying current and expected supporting technologies and skills. Expected supporting technologies and skills are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology and skills, and expected improvements/ trends. New technologies and skills will be tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in SV-8 milestones and linked to Enterprise Phases. SV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the Resources that constitute the Architecture. The SV-9 provides descriptions of relevant: emerging capabilities, industry trends, predictions of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software products, and current and possible future skills In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and products, the SV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture.

**Implementation**

SV-9 can be represented using a SV-9 table.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-9 Technology & Skills Forecast

**Related views**

The specific time periods selected (and the trends being tracked) will be coordinated with the Architecture transition plans (see SV-8). That is, insertion of new capabilities and upgrading / re-training of existing resources may depend on or be driven by the availability of new technology and associated skills.

If standards are an integral part of the technologies important to the evolution of a given Architecture, then it may be convenient to combine the SV-9 with the Technical Standards Forecast (TV-2).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Forecast
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Protocol
- Standard

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating SV-9 table

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Time Periods Dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Technology &amp; Skills Forecast defines the underlying current and expected supporting technologies and skills. Expected supporting technologies and skills are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology and skills, and expected improvements/ trends. New technologies and skills will be tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in SV-8 milestones and linked to Enterprise Phases. SV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the Resources that constitute the Architecture. The SV-9 provides descriptions of relevant: emerging capabilities, industry trends, predictions of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software products, and current and possible future skills In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and products, the SV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-9 can be represented using a SV-9 table.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sv_9.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="MODAF. SV-9 Technology &amp; Skills Forecast" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>SV-9 Technology &amp; Skills Forecast</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>The specific time periods selected (and the trends being tracked) will be coordinated with the Architecture transition plans (see SV-8). That is, insertion of new capabilities and upgrading / re-training of existing resources may depend on or be driven by the availability of new technology and associated skills.</p><p>If standards are an integral part of the technologies important to the evolution of a given Architecture, then it may be convenient to combine the SV-9 with the Technical Standards Forecast (TV-2).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85d87550-b43a-4e04-9ef1-234160dcca9a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Forecast</li><li>Resource Artifact</li><li>Software</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Organization</li><li>Post</li><li>Protocol</li><li>Standard</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d161b2a1-4a94-492b-9d2a-f4a492a32d0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating SV-9 table</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b975fd5-5418-466d-88b6-895f2747c97f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related references</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Time Periods Dialog</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575430 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01622: MODAF. TV-1 Standards Profile

- page_id: `249575430`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575430/MODAF.+TV-1+Standards+Profile
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.783+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Technical Standards Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A TV-1 defines the technical and non-technical standards, guidance, and policy applicable to the architecture.

As well as identifying applicable technical standards, the TV-1 may document the policies and standards that apply to the operational or business context.

**Implementation**

TV-1 can be represented by a TV-1 table.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### TV-1 Standards Profile

**Related views**

A TV-1 serves as the bridge between the SV and TV. The SV-9 forecasts relate to the TV-1 in that a timed technology forecast may contribute to the decision to retire or phase out the use of a certain standard in connection with a system element.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Standard
- Protocol

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating TV-1 table

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

TV-1 Standards Profile" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>TV-1 Standards Profile</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>A TV-1 serves as the bridge between the SV and TV. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575432 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01623: MODAF. TV-2 Standards Forecast

- page_id: `249575432`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575432/MODAF.+TV-2+Standards+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.820+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Technical Standards Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The forecast for evolutionary changes in the standards needs to be correlated against the time periods mentioned in the SV-8 and SV-9 views.

A Standards Forecast is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems and business processes covered by the architecture. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given architecture description is being built, and should identify issues that will affect the architecture.

A TV-2 complements and expands on the Standards Profile (TV-1) product and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture. For standards advice refer to the JSP 602 series of documents.

One of the prime purposes of this Product is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the Architecture and its constituent elements.

**Implementation**

TV-2 can be represented using a TV-2 table.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### TV-2 Standards Forecast

**Related views**

A TV-2 delineates the standards that will potentially impact the relevant system elements (from SV-1, SV-2, SV-4, SV-6, and OV-7) and relates them to the time periods that are listed in the SV-8 and SV-9. A system’s evolution, specified in the SV-8, may be tied to a future standard listed in the TV-2. A timed technology forecast from the SV9 is related to a TV-2 standards forecast in the following manner: a certain technology may be dependent on a TV2 standard (for example, a standard listed in TV-2 may not be adopted until a certain technology becomes available).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Standard
- Protocol
- Forecast

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating TV-2 table

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Time Periods Dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The forecast for evolutionary changes in the standards needs to be correlated against the time periods mentioned in the SV-8 and SV-9 views.</p><p>A Standards Forecast is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems and business processes covered by the architecture. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given architecture description is being built, and should identify issues that will affect the architecture.</p><p>A TV-2 complements and expands on the Standards Profile (TV-1) product and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture. For standards advice refer to the JSP 602 series of documents.</p><p>One of the prime purposes of this Product is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the Architecture and its constituent elements.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>TV-2 can be represented using a TV-2 table.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Tv_2.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="MODAF. TV-2 Standards Forecast" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>TV-2 Standards Forecast</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>A TV-2 delineates the standards that will potentially impact the relevant system elements (from SV-1, SV-2, SV-4, SV-6, and OV-7) and relates them to the time periods that are listed in the SV-8 and SV-9. A system’s evolution, specified in the SV-8, may be tied to a future standard listed in the TV-2. A timed technology forecast from the SV9 is related to a TV-2 standards forecast in the following manner: a certain technology may be dependent on a TV2 standard (for example, a standard listed in TV-2 may not be adopted until a certain technology becomes available).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="588d4794-1e31-43c5-bfa9-a4bcd20e04f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Standard</li><li>Protocol</li><li>Forecast</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5abc4ba6-7a82-4270-be6a-7903c9b6afb0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating TV-2 table</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d6c355d-ada1-4642-b4cf-f7531d8372fb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related references</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Time Periods Dialog</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576066 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01624: Modifying criteria through the legend

- page_id: `249576066`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576066/Modifying+criteria+through+the+legend
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-27T10:54:32.348+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Managing Traceability and Impact Analysis > Traceability relation maps
- labels: ['criteria-color', 'select-criteria', 'modify-criteria']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifying criteria through the legend' space='UPDM2P'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifying criteria through the legend' space='UPDM2P'] through the map legend.

colorTo change the criteria color

1. Double-click the Legend. The Relation Criteria dialog opens.
2. Select the criteria for which you want to change the color.
3. In the Color list, select the desired color.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

otherTo select other criteria

1. Double-click the Legend.
2. In the Relation Criteria dialog choose the Simple Navigation property group.
3. Click to clear the Show Relations Criteria Available Only for Context check-box. The list of available criteria is displayed.
4. Select the preferred criteria.
5. Click OK , when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can <ac:link ac:anchor="color"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[change the colors of the criteria relationships]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Modifying criteria through the legend" /></ac:link> and <ac:link ac:anchor="other"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[select other criteria]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Modifying criteria through the legend" /></ac:link> through the map legend.</p><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor"><ac:parameter ac:name="">color</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To change the criteria color</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the Legend. The <strong>Relation Criteria</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select the criteria for which you want to change the color.</li><li>In the <strong>Color</strong> list, select the desired color.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor"><ac:parameter ac:name="">other</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To select other criteria</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the Legend.</li><li>In the <strong>Relation Criteria</strong> dialog choose the <strong>Simple Navigation</strong> property group.</li><li>Click to clear the <strong>Show Relations Criteria Available Only for Context</strong> check-box. The list of available criteria is displayed.</li><li>Select the preferred criteria.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575127 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01625: Modifying CV-5 table

- page_id: `249575127`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575127/Modifying+CV-5+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:34:32.857+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping
- labels: ['remove-resources-from-cv-5', 'remove-capabilities-from-cv-5', 'modify-cv-5', 'add-capabilities-in-cv-5', 'add-resources-in-cv-5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1415794279

1415794281

1415794280

**To add/ remove Capabilities in/from a CV-5 table**

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to add or remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.A Capability of Performer relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.A Capability of Performer relationship between the context element and an added Capability is added in the model.

**To add/ remove Resources in/from a CV-5 table**

1. Click the cell you want to edit.
2. The + and - buttons appears at the right of the cell. [ATTACHMENT filename='Dr_table_remove_resources.png']
3. Do one of the following:
  - To add a Resource, select a Resource and click the + button . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deployment Milestones wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Deployment Milestones** wizard opens.
  - To remove a Resource, select a Resource and click the - button.

For more information about table management, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing Tables' space='UPDM2P'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575705 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01626: Modifying Dr table

- page_id: `249575705`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575705/Modifying+Dr+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-16T10:07:54.651+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Deployed Resources viewpoint > Dr Deployment Schedule
- labels: ['add-capabilities-in-dr', 'remove-capabilities-from-dr', 'remove-resources-from-dr', 'remove-actual-organizational-resources-from-dr', 'remove-actual-organizational-resources-from-model', 'manage-dr-table', 'modify-dr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add/remove Capabilities in/from Dr table

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to add or to remove them from the table.
3. Click OK when you are done.

- The Capabilities are removed only from the table but not from the model.
- An Exhibits relationship between the context element and Capability is added/removed from the model.

To remove Resources from Dr table

1. Click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select a Resource you want to remove and click the - button. [ATTACHMENT filename='Dr_table_remove_resources.png']

The Resource is removed only from the table but not from the model.

To remove Actual Organizational Resources from Dr table

1. Select Actual Organizational Resource.
2. Click Remove from Table button in table toolbar.

To remove Actual Organizational Resources from model

1. Select Actual Organizational Resource.
2. Click Delete button in table toolbar.

For more information about table management, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing Tables' space='UPDM2P'].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575539 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01628: NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views

- page_id: `249575539`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575539/NAF+4.0+viewpoints+and+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-17T11:16:56.533+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: ['naf-4', 'nato-architecture-framework']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NATO Architecture Framework (NAF) is a standard for developing Enterprise Architectures. An Enterprise Architecture is a model of a current or future state of an enterprise. An enterprise could be an organization, a system (including the human factors) or a project. The purpose of enterprise architecture is to capture the complex dependencies that exist in large-scale systems of systems so as to aid with decision support. NAF provides a standard way to model the architecture.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

800600

#### PANEL: NAF 4.0 viewpoints

NAF 4.0 viewpoints

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

An Enterprise Architecture is a model of a current or future state of an enterprise. An enterprise could be an organization, a <span class="wp-glossary wpg-tooltip">system</span> (including the human factors) or a project. The purpose of enterprise architecture is to capture the complex dependencies that exist in large-scale systems of systems so as to aid with decision support. NAF provides a standard way to model the architecture.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="NAF 4.0 Viewpoints and Views" ac:title="NAF 4.0 Viewpoints and Views"><ri:attachment ri:filename="viewpoints-views_naf4.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="a8f2f393-ea58-4796-9db9-ff07eddbf5c6" ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">800</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9amaJGkw9xk" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">600</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="71e38be2-9fd3-421a-82b6-e94766f454bd" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">NAF 4.0 viewpoints</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="f75fe6f2-78a5-4558-a8bd-70f83ce7344e" ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575435 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01629: NATO All View Viewpoint

- page_id: `249575435`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575435/NATO+All+View+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.625+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are some overarching aspects of architectures that relate to all views. These overarching aspects are captured in the NATO All View (NAV). The NAV products provide information pertinent to the entire architecture but do not represent a distinct view of the architecture. NAV subviews set the scope and context of the architecture. The scope includes the subject area and timeframe for the architecture. The setting in which the architecture exists comprises the interrelated conditions that compose the context for the architecture. These conditions include doctrine, tactics, techniques and procedures, relevant goals and vision statements, concepts of operations, scenarios, and environmental conditions.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>There are some overarching aspects of architectures that relate to all views. These overarching aspects are captured in the NATO All View (NAV). The NAV products provide information pertinent to the entire architecture but do not represent a distinct view of the architecture. NAV subviews set the scope and context of the architecture. The scope includes the subject area and timeframe for the architecture. The setting in which the architecture exists comprises the interrelated conditions that compose the context for the architecture. These conditions include doctrine, tactics, techniques and procedures, relevant goals and vision statements, concepts of operations, scenarios, and environmental conditions.</p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="e4daac0e-c219-49ec-a536-ac0d091ebc35" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575441 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01630: NATO Capability Viewpoint

- page_id: `249575441`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575441/NATO+Capability+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.690+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NATO Capability View (NCV) supports the process of analyzing and optimizing the delivery of military capabilities in line with NATO‘s strategic intent. The NCV achieves this by capturing essential elements of NATO‘s strategic vision and concepts and NATO‘s capability planning process, and decomposing this data into a capability taxonomy. The taxonomy is augmented with schedule data and measures of effectiveness to enable the analysis of capability gaps and overlaps. The NCV further details the dependencies between military capabilities, enabling 
capability options to be built in a more coherent manner and effective trade-offs to be conducted across NATO common funded programmes.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The NATO Capability View (NCV) supports the process of analyzing and optimizing the delivery of military capabilities in line with NATO‘s strategic intent. The NCV achieves this by capturing essential elements of NATO‘s strategic vision and concepts and NATO‘s capability planning process, and decomposing this data into a capability taxonomy. The taxonomy is augmented with schedule data and measures of effectiveness to enable the analysis of capability gaps and overlaps. The NCV further details the dependencies between military capabilities, enabling<br />capability options to be built in a more coherent manner and effective trade-offs to be conducted across NATO common funded programmes.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bdbfe801-97db-4028-b95f-fb30b8478177"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xD91EYVU-bM" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>  <ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c662a95d-1b50-48a6-bd99-a750714426b4"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LB1Ure3ravQ" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><br /></p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="eaffc0e1-cfb5-4837-bc3e-4b9daf59bad7" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575466 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01631: NATO Operational Viewpoint

- page_id: `249575466`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575466/NATO+Operational+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.960+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NOV is a description of the tasks and activities, operational elements, and information exchanges required to accomplish NATO missions. The NOV contains graphical and textual products that comprise an identification of the nodes, their assigned tasks and activities, and dependencies between nodes. It defines the types of information exchanged, which tasks and activities are supported by the information exchanges, and the operational details of information exchanges.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The NOV is a description of the tasks and activities, operational elements, and information exchanges required to accomplish NATO missions. The NOV contains graphical and textual products that comprise an identification of the nodes, their assigned tasks and activities, and dependencies between nodes. It defines the types of information exchanged, which tasks and activities are supported by the information exchanges, and the operational details of information exchanges.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="99b8c7c0-49af-4ecc-929f-17777e8e052a"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jrpPuZ8Vf74" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f9c5597d-1c9b-46ac-b11b-bb539523bda6" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575460 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01632: NATO Programme Viewpoint

- page_id: `249575460`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575460/NATO+Programme+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.894+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Programme views are introduced in the NAF Version 3 to describe the relationships between NATO capability requirements and the various programmes and projects being implemented. They provide programmatic details 
and highlight the dependencies between capability management and the NATO acquisition process. This information can be further leveraged to show the impact of acquisition decisions on the architecture.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Programme views are introduced in the NAF Version 3 to describe the relationships between NATO capability requirements and the various programmes and projects being implemented. They provide programmatic details<br />and highlight the dependencies between capability management and the NATO acquisition process. This information can be further leveraged to show the impact of acquisition decisions on the architecture.</p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="9ea9c0fe-d4ae-4812-a077-9efc19352422" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575487 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01633: NATO Service-Oriented Viewpoint

- page_id: `249575487`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575487/NATO+Service-Oriented+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:43.241+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NSOV is added to the NAF to support building architectures based on the concept of a Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA), which is fundamental to the NNEC paradigm. The NSOV is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the NATO Operational View. A service, within the NSOV, is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer. NSOV focuses strictly on identifying and describing services. The view also supports the description of service taxonomies, service orchestrations, a mapping of services to operational activities, and a description of various forms of service behaviour.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The NSOV is added to the NAF to support building architectures based on the concept of a Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA), which is fundamental to the NNEC paradigm. The NSOV is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the NATO Operational View. A service, within the NSOV, is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer. NSOV focuses strictly on identifying and describing services. The view also supports the description of service taxonomies, service orchestrations, a mapping of services to operational activities, and a description of various forms of service behaviour.</p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="cae37911-de3e-495e-8809-d1f9df74fe9b" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575499 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01634: NATO Systems Viewpoint

- page_id: `249575499`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575499/NATO+Systems+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:43.416+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NSV is a set of graphical and textual products that describes systems and interconnections providing for or supporting NATO processes. The NSV associates systems resources to the NOV and/or the NSOV. These systems are the resources that are used to construct the services of the NSOV, and support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information among operational nodes as defined in the NOV.

The views of this view are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The NSV is a set of graphical and textual products that describes systems and interconnections providing for or supporting NATO processes. The NSV associates systems resources to the NOV and/or the NSOV. These systems are the resources that are used to construct the services of the NSOV, and support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information among operational nodes as defined in the NOV.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f2d8bd4-b5bf-4e5b-b114-1604a594cf3c"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qvL33ckmJJY" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>The views of this view are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="2c343f17-2404-438d-8e86-392002f17168" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575534 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01635: NATO Technical Viewpoint

- page_id: `249575534`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575534/NATO+Technical+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:44.018+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NTV provides the technical implementation guidelines upon which engineering specifications are based and common building blocks are established. The NTV includes a collection of the technical standards, implementation conventions, standards options, rules, and criteria organized into profiles that govern systems and services, and their elements for a given architecture.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The NTV provides the technical implementation guidelines upon which engineering specifications are based and common building blocks are established. The NTV includes a collection of the technical standards, implementation conventions, standards options, rules, and criteria organized into profiles that govern systems and services, and their elements for a given architecture.</p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="d79a3e72-ee4e-4961-b192-01975c9717f7" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575436 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01636: NAV-1 Overview and Summary Information

- page_id: `249575436`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575436/NAV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.634+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO All View Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The overview and summary information contained within the NAV-1 product provides an executive-level summary information in a consistent form that allows for quick reference and comparison between architectural descriptions. NAV-1 includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture. In an enterprise repository environment, individual architectures are mapped against enterprise phases to provide context between the architectures. NAV-1 is usually a structured text product. Organizations may create a template for the NAV-1 that can then be used to create a consistent set of information across different architecture-based projects.

**Implementation**

NAV-1 can be represented:

- Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial NAV-1 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.
- Using a NAV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fragment of NAV-1 Overview and Summary Information report

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Architectural Description
- Defines Architecture
- Architecture Metadata

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Generating reports

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The overview and summary information contained within the NAV-1 product provides an executive-level summary information in a consistent form that allows for quick reference and comparison between architectural descriptions. NAV-1 includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture. In an enterprise repository environment, individual architectures are mapped against enterprise phases to provide context between the architectures. NAV-1 is usually a structured text product. Organizations may create a template for the NAV-1 that can then be used to create a consistent set of information across different architecture-based projects.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>NAV-1 can be represented:</p><ul><li>Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial NAV-1 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.</li><li>Using a NAV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Nav_1.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="NAV-1 Overview and Summary Information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Fragment of NAV-1 Overview and Summary Information report</h6></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="663a06c2-e5d2-4444-93df-5ee25e61bb7c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Architectural Description</li><li>Defines Architecture</li><li>Architecture Metadata</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b680ae1c-ec40-4a7d-99fa-26c010ecba2e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Generating reports</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575438 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01637: NAV-2 Integrated Dictionary

- page_id: `249575438`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575438/NAV-2+Integrated+Dictionary
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.656+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO All View Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NAV-2 presents all the metadata used in an architecture. An NAV-2 presents all the data as a hierarchy and provides a text definition for each one by displaying the following properties:

- The name used for this element in the architecture
- Alternative names for this element, for example, if the element is listed in the NATO ontology it may have multiple names
- A brief description of the element

**Implementation**

NAV-2 can be represented:

- Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data.
- An AV-2 table. If you have NAV-2 diagrams from previous versions, they will bemigrated into the NAV-1 diagram when loading the project with UPDM 17.0.2.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NAV-2 Integrated Dictionary

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fragment of NAV-2 Integrated Dictionary report

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Definition
- Alias
- Same As

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Generating reports

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575442 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01638: NCV-1 Capability Vision

- page_id: `249575442`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575442/NCV-1+Capability+Vision
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.698+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

NCV-1 addresses the enterprise concerns associated with the overall vision for transformational endeavors and thus defines the strategic context for a group of Enterprise capabilities. The purpose of an NCV-1 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the Architecture. It also provides a high-level scope for the Architecture that is more general than the scenario-based scope defined in an NOV-1. The Views are high-level and describe capabilities using terminology that is easily understood by non-technical readers (though they may make extensive use of military terminology and acronyms that are clearly defined in the NAV-2 View). It should be noted that visions for capabilities of the enterprise should therefore be placed within entities stereotyped as «EnterpriseVision».

**Implementation**

NCV-1 can be represented using a NCV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### NCV-1 Capability Vision

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Enterprise Goal
- Enterprise Vision
- Enterprise Phase
- Whole-Life Enterprise
- Structural Part
- Temporal Part
- Enduring Task
- Capability
- Exhibits

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating NCV-1 diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>NCV-1 addresses the enterprise concerns associated with the overall vision for transformational endeavors and thus defines the strategic context for a group of Enterprise capabilities. The purpose of an NCV-1 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the Architecture. It also provides a high-level scope for the Architecture that is more general than the scenario-based scope defined in an NOV-1. The Views are high-level and describe capabilities using terminology that is easily understood by non-technical readers (though they may make extensive use of military terminology and acronyms that are clearly defined in the NAV-2 View). It should be noted that visions for capabilities of the enterprise should therefore be placed within entities stereotyped as «EnterpriseVision».</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>NCV-1 can be represented using a NCV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ncv_1.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="NCV-1 Capability Vision" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><h6>NCV-1 Capability Vision</h6></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d95cbe79-ec41-4f15-a2f4-16d0cd3e81a9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Enterprise Goal</li><li>Enterprise Vision</li><li>Enterprise Phase</li><li>Whole-Life Enterprise</li><li>Structural Part</li><li>Temporal Part</li><li>Enduring Task</li><li>Capability</li><li>Exhibits</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a6b66700-a15f-4d58-8472-a256f4e8a2b1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating NCV-1 diagram</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575444 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01639: NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy

- page_id: `249575444`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575444/NCV-2+Capability+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.719+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NCV-2 Product models capability taxonomies. The view presents a hierarchy of capabilities. These capabilities may be presented in context of an Enterprise Phase, for example, it can show the required capabilities for current and future enterprises.

**Implementation**

NCV-2 can be represented using a NCV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

If your project has migrated from any earlier than 17.0.1 version, the oldNCV-5 View representation based on the UML Class diagram is loaded as the NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Exhibits
- Climate
- Environment
- Environment Property
- Light Condition
- Location

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating NCV-2 diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The NCV-2 Product models capability taxonomies. The view presents a hierarchy of capabilities. These capabilities may be presented in context of an Enterprise Phase, for example, it can show the required capabilities for current and future enterprises.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>NCV-2 can be represented using a NCV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97649483-0ef9-4a78-8af1-23c3eeddb245"><ac:rich-text-body>If your project has migrated from any earlier than 17.0.1 version, the oldNCV-5 View representation based on the UML Class diagram is loaded as the NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ncv_2.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy</h6></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="212eb01a-0ffd-4337-8280-0cedd6137f98"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capability</li><li>Exhibits</li><li>Climate</li><li>Environment</li><li>Environment Property</li><li>Light Condition</li><li>Location</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="90bdd683-4b59-443d-96a5-b11a981ccc00"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating NCV-2 diagram</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575446 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01640: NCV-3 Capability Phasing

- page_id: `249575446`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575446/NCV-3+Capability+Phasing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.742+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

NCV-3 addresses the planned achievement of capability at different points in time or during specific periods of time, i.e. capability phasing. NCV-3 Views support the Capability Audit process by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. The view indicates capability increments, which should be associated with delivery milestones within acquisition projects (when the increments are associated with capability deliveries).

**Implementation**

NCV-3 can be represented using a NCV-3 diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### NCV-3 Capability Phasing****

**Related views**

The View is created by analyzing programmatic project data to determine when the projects providing elements of military capability are to be delivered, upgraded and/or withdrawn (this data may be provided in part by a Programme Timelines (NPV-2) View). Then the capability increments identified are structured according to the required capabilities determined in the Capability Taxonomy (NCV-2) View and the Enterprise Phases (from NCV1).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Capability Configuration
- Exhibits
- Increment Milestone
- Out Of Service Milestone
- Actual Project

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>NCV-3 addresses the planned achievement of capability at different points in time or during specific periods of time, i.e. capability phasing. NCV-3 Views support the Capability Audit process by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. The view indicates capability increments, which should be associated with delivery milestones within acquisition projects (when the increments are associated with capability deliveries).</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>NCV-3 can be represented using a NCV-3 diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ncv_3.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="NCV-3 Capability Phasing" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><h6>NCV-3 Capability Phasing<strong><br /></strong></h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>The View is created by analyzing programmatic project data to determine when the projects providing elements of military capability are to be delivered, upgraded and/or withdrawn (this data may be provided in part by a Programme Timelines (NPV-2) View). Then the capability increments identified are structured according to the required capabilities determined in the Capability Taxonomy (NCV-2) View and the Enterprise Phases (from NCV1).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9afaa9f-77a4-4c7c-9c8f-acb8c1373694"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capability</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Exhibits</li><li>Increment Milestone</li><li>Out Of Service Milestone</li><li>Actual Project</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575448 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01641: NCV-4 Capability Dependencies

- page_id: `249575448`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575448/NCV-4+Capability+Dependencies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.762+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NCV-4 Product describes the dependencies between planned capabilities. It also defines logical groupings of capabilities (capability clusters). The NCV-4 View provides a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities. The groupings of capabilities are logical, and the purpose of the groupings is to guide enterprise management.

**Implementation**

NCV-4 can be represented using a NCV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NCV-4 Capability Dependencies

**Related views**

The Capability Dependencies (NCV-4) View describes the relationships between capabilities (capabilities may be reused from an NCV-2). It also defines logical groupings of capabilities. This contrasts with the NCV-2 which also deals with relationships between Capabilities, but the NCV-2 only addresses the specialization-generalization relationship (for example, capability taxonomy).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Capability Property

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating NCV-4 diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The NCV-4 Product describes the dependencies between planned capabilities. It also defines logical groupings of capabilities (capability clusters). The NCV-4 View provides a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities. The groupings of capabilities are logical, and the purpose of the groupings is to guide enterprise management.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>NCV-4 can be represented using a NCV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ncv_4.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="NCV-4 Capability Dependencies" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>NCV-4 Capability Dependencies</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>The Capability Dependencies (NCV-4) View describes the relationships between capabilities (capabilities may be reused from an NCV-2). It also defines logical groupings of capabilities. This contrasts with the NCV-2 which also deals with relationships between Capabilities, but the NCV-2 only addresses the specialization-generalization relationship (for example, capability taxonomy).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2fb71c80-1d21-4ab4-833f-eb77f1a1827e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capability</li><li>Capability Property</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9b22b188-6698-45ce-8e03-960d969aad61"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating NCV-4 diagram</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575450 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01642: NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping

- page_id: `249575450`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575450/NCV-5+Capability+to+Organisational+Deployment+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.784+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NCV-5 defines Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping. It addresses the fulfillment of capability requirements, in particular by network enabled capabilities. The NCV-5 View is used to support the capability management process and, in particular, assist the planning of fielding.

**Implementation**

NCV-5 can be represented using:

- A NCV-5 table. 
 If your project has migrated from any earlier than 17.0.1 version, theold NCV-5 View representation based on the UML Class diagram is loaded as the NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram.
- A NCV-5 spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NCV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping

| Icon | View | Relationship |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | Capability Phasing is defined in NCV-1. |
|  |  | Capabilities shown in NCV-5 table are usually modeled in NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy. |
|  |  | The timing is based on project milestones from NPV1 which indicate when capability configuration will be delivered to an organization. |
|  |  | Actual Organizational Resources related to deployment of Capability Configurations are taken from NOV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart. |

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Capability Configuration
- Exhibits
- Actual Organization
- Actual Post
- Deployed Milestone
- No Longer Used Milestone
- Actual Project
- Enterprise Phase
- Whole-Life Enterprise

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating NCV-5 table
- Modifying NCV-5 table

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Deployment Milestones Creation Wizard

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575456 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01643: NCV-6 Capability to Operational Activity Mapping

- page_id: `249575456`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575456/NCV-6+Capability+to+Operational+Activity+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.852+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NCV-6 Product describes the mapping between the capabilities required by an Enterprise and the operational activities that those capabilities support.

**Implementation**

NCV-6 can be represented using a NCV-6 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Capabilities will be used as row elements and the Operational Activities will be used as column elements.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NCV-6 Capability to Operational Activity Mapping

**Related views**

The NCV-6 View provides a bridge between capability analyses using NCVs and operational activities analyzed using NOVs. Specifically, it identifies how operational activities can be performed using various available capability elements. It is similar in function to the NSV-5 which maps system functions to operational activities.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Standard Operational Activity
- Maps to Capability

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Building NCV-6 matrix

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 01644: NCV-7 Capability to Service Mapping

- page_id: `249575458`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575458/NCV-7+Capability+to+Service+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.873+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NCV-7 Product describes the mapping between the capabilities required by an Enterprise and services as defined for SOA.

**Implementation**

NCV-7 can be represented using a NCV-6 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Service Interfaces will be used as the row elements and the Capabilities will be used as the column elements.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NCV-7 Capability to Service Mapping

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Interface
- Expose
- Capability

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Building NCV-7 matrix

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1097310434

1097310437

1097310436

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: NA

DoDAF: A needline documents the requirement to exchange information between nodes. The needline does not indicate how the information transfer is implemented.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| exchangedItem : NeedlineExchange[*] | An exchange that occurs on the Needline. |

##### Extensions

UML Association, UML Connector
