<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575164 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01701: OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Internal Description

- page_id: `249575164`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575164/OV-2+Operational+Resource+Flow+Internal+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:24:36.415+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-2-operational-resource-flow-internal-description', 'ov-2-internal']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

49817542

49817544

49817543

**Description**

A specific application of the Operational Resource Flow Internal Description is to describe a logical pattern of resource (information, funding, personnel, or materiel) internal flows.

**Implementation**

OV-2 can be represented using:

- An OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- An OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Needlines established in an OV-2 can be realized by resources and their interactions in a SV-1 Systems Interface Description model or SvcV-1 Services Context Description model.

In developing an Architectural Description, OV-2 and OV-5b Operational Activity Model are often the starting points and these may be developed iteratively. OV-2 focuses on the Operational Resource Flows, with the activities being a secondary adornment. The OV-5b, on the other hand, places first-order attention on operational activities and only second-order attention on Resource Flows, which can be shown as annotations or swim lanes on the activities.

49817541

OV-2 focuses on the Operational Resource Flows, with the activities being a secondary adornment. The OV-5b, on the other hand, places first-order attention on operational activities and only second-order attention on Resource Flows, which can be shown as annotations or swim lanes on the activities.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fcdd8703-9712-442c-9f41-ab6d2bfe1470"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">49817541</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Related Pages:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="92457bcf-9bc3-4a90-94be-b40aa96273b8" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Node Role" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Needline" /></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 01702: OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix

- page_id: `249575168`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

898739316

898739318

898739317

**Description**

The OV-3 addresses operational Resource Flows exchanged between Operational Activities and locations.

Resource Flows provide further detail of the interoperability requirements associated with the operational capability of interest. The focus is on Resource Flows that cross the capability boundary.

The intended usage of the OV-3 includes definition of interoperability requirements.

**Implementation**

OV-3 can be represented using:

- An OV-3 table.
- An OV-3 role-based table. A role-based table represents exchanges between Node Roles (performer usages).
- An OV-3 spreadsheet report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

898739315

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575171 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01703: OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart

- page_id: `249575171`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575171/OV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:25:37.714+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-4-organizational-relationships-chart', 'ov-4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

899477282

899477285

899477283

**Description**

The OV-4 shows organizational structures and interactions. The organizations shown may be civil or military. The OV-4 exists in two forms; role-based (e.g., a typical brigade command structure) and actual (e.g., an organization chart for a department or agency).

A role-based OV-4 shows the possible relationships between organizational resources. The key relationship is composition, i.e., one organizational resource being part of a parent organization. In addition to this, the architect may show the roles each organizational resource has, and the interactions between those roles, i.e., the roles represent the functional aspects of organizational resources. There are no prescribed resource interactions in DoDAF V2.0: the architect should select an appropriate interaction type from the DM2 or add a new one. Interactions illustrate the fundamental roles and management responsibilities, such as supervisory reporting, Command and Control (C2) relationships, collaboration and so on.

An actual OV-4 shows the structure of a real organization at a particular point in time, and is used to provide context to other parts of the architecture such as AV-1 and the CVs.

The intended usage of the role-based OV-4 includes:

- Organizational analysis.
- Definition of human roles.
- Operational analysis.

The intended usage of the actual OV-4 includes:

- Identify architecture stakeholders.
- Identify process owners.
- Illustrate current or future organization structures.

**Implementation**

OV-4 can be represented using:

- An OV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The organizations that are modeled using OV-4 may also appear in other models, for example in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P'] as organizational constituents of a capability or a resource and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships' space='UPDM2P'] where organizations own projects. In a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P'], for instance, the organizational resources defined in a typical OV-4 may be part of a capability or resources. Also, actual organizations may form elements of a fielded capability which realizes the requirements at the system-level (again, this may be depicted on a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P']).

899477280

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Person Type' space='UPDM2P']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Skill' space='UPDM2P']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Individual Person Role' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Organization Relationship' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Organization Role' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='UPDM2P']

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67cab179-226f-476c-8fed-dbbc9de5d9d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">899477282</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2781dd52-b08f-4a39-8bd7-91f04a2871a0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">899477285</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58e3b526-95a4-4d7b-9d1b-1fe48743e69f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">899477283</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The OV-4 shows organizational structures and interactions. The organizations shown may be civil or military. The OV-4 exists in two forms; role-based (e.g., a typical brigade command structure) and actual (e.g., an organization chart for a department or agency).</p><p>A role-based OV-4 shows the possible relationships between organizational resources. The key relationship is composition, i.e., one organizational resource being part of a parent organization. In addition to this, the architect may show the roles each organizational resource has, and the interactions between those roles, i.e., the roles represent the functional aspects of organizational resources. There are no prescribed resource interactions in DoDAF V2.0: the architect should select an appropriate interaction type from the DM2 or add a new one. Interactions illustrate the fundamental roles and management responsibilities, such as supervisory reporting, Command and Control (C2) relationships, collaboration and so on.</p><p>An actual OV-4 shows the structure of a real organization at a particular point in time, and is used to provide context to other parts of the architecture such as AV-1 and the CVs.</p><p>The intended usage of the role-based OV-4 includes:</p><ul><li>Organizational analysis.</li><li>Definition of human roles.</li><li>Operational analysis.</li></ul><p>The intended usage of the actual OV-4 includes:</p><ul><li>Identify architecture stakeholders.</li><li>Identify process owners.</li><li>Illustrate current or future organization structures.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>OV-4 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>An OV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>A UML Class diagram.</li><li>A SysML Block Definition diagram.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" ac:alt="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart"><ri:attachment ri:filename="OV-4 Typical.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The organizations that are modeled using OV-4 may also appear in other models, for example in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /></ac:link> as organizational constituents of a capability or a resource and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships" /></ac:link> where organizations own projects. In a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /></ac:link>, for instance, the organizational resources defined in a typical OV-4 may be part of a capability or resources. Also, actual organizations may form elements of a fielded capability which realizes the requirements at the system-level (again, this may be depicted on a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /></ac:link>).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3fdd0d06-c225-4534-9862-41230cc75648"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">899477280</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="b4758258-7ac7-4d66-8cab-9b1e7d3a2938" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Instantiating Structures" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Organization Type" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Person Type" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Command" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Skill" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Skill of Person Type" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Skill Of Person Type]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Individual Person Role" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Actual Organization Relationship" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Actual Organization Role" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575174 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01704: OV-5 Operational Activity Model

- page_id: `249575174`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575174/OV-5+Operational+Activity+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:10:29.720+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-5', 'ov-5a-operational-activity-decomposition-tree', 'ov-5b-operational-activity-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1425992109

1425992112

1425992111

**Description**

The OV-5a and the OV-5b describe the operations that are normally conducted in the course of achieving a mission or a business goal. It describes operational activities (or tasks); Input/Output flows between activities, and to/from activities that are outside the scope of the Architectural Description.

The OV-5a and OV-5b describes the operational activities that are being conducted within the mission or scenario.

The OV-5a and OV-5b can be used to:

- Clearly delineate lines of responsibility for activities when coupled with OV-2.
- Uncover unnecessary Operational Activity redundancy.
- Make decisions about streamlining, combining, or omitting activities.
- Define or flag issues, opportunities, or operational activities and their interactions (information flows among the activities) that need to be scrutinized further.
- Provide a necessary foundation for depicting activity sequencing and timing in the OV-6a Operational Rules Model, the OV-6b State Transition Description, and the OV-6c Event-Trace Description.

The OV-5b describes the operational, business, and defense portion of the intelligence community activities associated with the Architectural Description, as well as the:

- Relationships or dependencies among the activities.
- Resources exchanged between activities.
- External interchanges (from/to business activities that are outside the scope of the model).

An Operational Activity is what work is required, specified independently of how it is carried out. To maintain this independence from implementation, logical activities and locations in OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description are used to represent the structure which carries out the Operational Activities. Operational Activities are realized as System Functions (described in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description) or Service Functions (described in SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description) which are the how to the Operational Activities what, i.e., they are specified in terms of the resources that carry them out.

The intended usage of the OV-5a and OV-5b includes:

- Description of activities and workflows.
- Requirements capture.
- Definition of roles and responsibilities.
- Support task analysis to determine training needs.
- Problem space definition.
- Operational planning.
- Logistic support analysis.
- Information flow analysis.

**Implementation**

OV-5 can be represented using:

- An OV-5a diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='OV-5a.png']
- An OV-5b diagram for Operational Activity flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='OV-5b.png']
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Activity diagram.
- A SysML Block diagram.
- A SysML Activity diagram.

You can also create OV-5 Business Process Diagram (BPD). In order to do that, use the Cameo Business Modeler plugin.

The OV-5s and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P'] model are, to a degree, complements of each other. The OV-5s focuses on the operational activities whereas [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P'] model focuses on the operational activities in relation to locations. Due to the relationship between locations and operational activities, these types of models should normally be developed together.

1425992107

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575180 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01705: OV-5 Operational Activity Model BPD

- page_id: `249575180`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575180/OV-5+Operational+Activity+Model+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:39:07.752+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Operational viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

291401905

291401907

291401906

OV-5 Operational Activity Model (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

OV-5 Operational Activity Model (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

Some diagram elements have both UPDM and BPMN stereotypes.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575181 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01706: OV-6a Operational Rules Model

- page_id: `249575181`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575181/OV-6a+Operational+Rules+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:28:44.279+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-6a-operational-rules-model', 'ov-6a']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

382653897

382653899

382653898

**Description**

An OV-6a specifies operational or business rules that are constraints on the way that business is done in the enterprise. At a top-level, rules should at least embody the concepts of operations defined in OV-1 High Level Operational Concept Graphic and provide guidelines for the development and definition of more detailed rules and behavioral definitions that should occur later in the Architectural definition process.

The intended usage of the OV-6a includes:

- Definition of doctrinally correct operational procedures.
- Definition of business rules.
- Identification of operational constraints.

**Implementation**

OV-6a can be represented using:

- An OV-6a table. [ATTACHMENT filename='OV-6a.PNG']
- An OV-6a spreadsheet report.

Rules defined in an OV-6a may optionally be presented in any other OV. For example, a rule “battle damage assessment shall be carried out under fair weather conditions” may be linked to the Conduct BDA activity in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='UPDM2P']. Any natural language rule presented (e.g., in a diagram note) should also be listed in OV-6a.

382653896

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Constraint' space='UPDM2P']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Performer' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Mission' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='UPDM2P']

At a top-level, rules should at least embody the concepts of operations defined in OV-1 High Level Operational Concept Graphic and provide guidelines for the development and definition of more detailed rules and behavioral definitions that should occur later in the Architectural definition process.</p><p>The intended usage of the OV-6a includes:</p><ul><li>Definition of doctrinally correct operational procedures.</li><li>Definition of business rules.</li><li>Identification of operational constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>OV-6a can be represented using:</p><ul><li>An OV-6a table.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="OV-6a Operational Rules Model" ac:alt="OV-6a Operational Rules Model"><ri:attachment ri:filename="OV-6a.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="OV-6a Operational Rules Model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>An OV-6a spreadsheet report.</li></ul><p>Rules defined in an OV-6a may optionally be presented in any other OV. For example, a rule “battle damage assessment shall be carried out under fair weather conditions” may be linked to the Conduct BDA activity in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Any natural language rule presented (e.g., in a diagram note) should also be listed in OV-6a.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dafbdf36-9c51-4c94-a2ad-6e38bb233b6c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">382653896</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="a564a7f8-b644-4779-a080-3823ac37b510" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Operational Constraint" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Entity Item" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Exchange Element" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Performer" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Mission" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Operational Exchange" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575184 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01707: OV-6b Operational State Transition Description

- page_id: `249575184`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575184/OV-6b+Operational+State+Transition+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-09-07T18:46:42.548+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-6b-operational-state-transition-description', 'ov-6b']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

382941516

382941518

382941517

**Description**

The OV-6b is a graphical method of describing how an Operational Activity responds to various events by changing its state. The diagram represents the sets of events to which the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

An OV-6b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. The OV-6b is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in the OV-5b Operational Activity Model. The OV-6b relates events and states. A change of state is called a transition. Actions may be associated with a given state or with the transition between states in response to stimuli (e.g., triggers and events).

The intended usage of the OV-6b includes:

- Analysis of business events.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of constraints.

**Implementation**

OV-6b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The OV-6b reflects the fact that the explicit sequencing of activities in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram' space='UPDM2P'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='UPDM2P'].

382941515

**Related Pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational State Description' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational State' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Performer' space='UPDM2P']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f02cc2f-b378-424f-89ea-9e23316bdf6f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">382941516</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c23f6de-f710-4819-8f09-8d528f85653a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">382941518</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8a3d707-130c-46a6-a085-47e259944d75"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">382941517</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The OV-6b is a graphical method of describing how an Operational Activity responds to various events by changing its state. The diagram represents the sets of events to which the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.</p><p>An OV-6b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. The OV-6b is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in the OV-5b Operational Activity Model. The OV-6b relates events and states. A change of state is called a transition. Actions may be associated with a given state or with the transition between states in response to stimuli (e.g., triggers and events).</p><p>The intended usage of the OV-6b includes:</p><ul><li>Analysis of business events.</li><li>Behavioral analysis.</li><li>Identification of constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>OV-6b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="OV-6b Operational State Transition Description" ac:alt="OV-6b Operational State Transition Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="OV-6b.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="OV-6b Operational State Transition Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The OV-6b reflects the fact that the explicit sequencing of activities in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5a Operational Activity Decomposition Tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b Operational Activity Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b764ab0-59f4-46dd-898b-715747935134"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">382941515</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Operational State Description" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Operational State" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Performer" /></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575186 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01708: OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description

- page_id: `249575186`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575186/OV-6c+Operational+Event-Trace+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:42:36.027+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-6c-operational-event-trace-description', 'ov-6c']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

383506446

383506448

383506447

**Description**

The OV-6c provides a time-ordered examination of the Resource Flows as a result of a particular scenario. Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

Operational Event/Trace Descriptions, sometimes called sequence diagrams, event scenarios, or timing diagrams, allow the tracing of actions in a scenario or critical sequence of events. The OV-6c can be used by itself or in conjunction with an OV-6b State Transition Description to describe the dynamic behavior of activities.

The intended usage of the OV-6c includes:

- Analysis of operational events.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional user requirements.
- Operational test scenarios.

**Implementation**

OV-6c can be represented using a UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The information content of messages in an OV-6c may be related with the Resource Flows in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='UPDM2P'] and information entities in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space='UPDM2P'].

383506445

**Related Pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Event Trace' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Message' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Performer' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Node Role' space='UPDM2P']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575189 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01709: OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description BPD

- page_id: `249575189`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575189/OV-6c+Operational+Event-Trace+Description+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:10:10.632+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Operational viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

383707028

383707031

383707030

OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities. An OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). Some diagram elements have both UPDM and BPMN stereotypes.

383707026

**Related Pages:**

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational viewpoint' space='UPDM2P']

- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Operational Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1099364048

1099364050

1099364049

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: The OwnsProcess (MODAF::ProcessOwner) relationship asserts that an ActualOrganizationalResource has responsibility for an OperationalActivity.

This does not imply the resource conducts the activity, merely that it has managerial responsibility for it.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Dependency

##### Related DoDAF views

[OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/OV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/MODAF.+OV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart)

##### Related NAF views

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575645 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01712: P1 Resource Types

- page_id: `249575645`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575645/P1+Resource+Types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-07T11:21:15.659+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p1-resource-types', 'p1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The P1 Resource Types view specifies the types of Resources, Technology and Competences required for the architecture. It can also be used to specify properties of Resources. This view collects together all the Resource Types in the architecture. It can also be used to identify Technologies and Competences and map these to resource types. Technologies and Competences can also be set against a timeline indicating when they are expected to be in use.

The P1 view may be used for:

- Identifying Resource Taxonomies.
- Forecasting technology readiness against time.
- HR trends analysis.
- Recruitment planning.
- Planning technology insertion.
- Input to options analysis.
- Definition of performance characteristics.
- Identification of non-functional requirements (input to SRD).

**Implementation**

The P1 view can be represented using:

- A P1 Resource Types diagram. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- A P1 Systems to Systems Matrix. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- A P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table. [ATTACHMENT filename='P1_typical.png']
- A P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements table. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- A P1 Resource Types Technology Forecast table. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- A P1 Resource Types Service Provision which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The ServiceInterfaces are used as the row elements and the Resources are used as the column elements. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Resource Types diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Interaction in P1 diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements table' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating P1 Actual table from P1 Typical table' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Systems to Systems Matrix' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Technology Forecast table' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Service Provision matrix' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating document based reports' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Resource Role
- Resource Interface
- Resource Port
- Resource Connector
- Capability
- Exhibits
- Fielded Capability
- Service
- Service Interface
- Request
- Resource Interaction
- Control
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel
- Measurement Set
- Measurement
- Actual Measurement
- Actual Property Set
- Forecast
- Protocol
- Standard

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575660 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01713: P2 Resource Structure

- page_id: `249575660`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575660/P2+Resource+Structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:41:13.605+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p2-resource-structure', 'p2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The P2 Resource Structure view addresses the composition and (high-level) interaction of resources.

The P2 view links together the operational and systems architecture views by depicting how resources are structured and interact in order to realize the logical architecture specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Scenario' space='UPDM2P']. The P2 view may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Scenario' space='UPDM2P'] view(i.e. in a to-be architecture) and so there may be many alternative Resource view suites that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an as-is architecture, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Scenario' space='UPDM2P']view may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the P2 to allow communication of key information flows to non-technical stakeholders.

The P2 view can be used to specify typical (or template) organization structures, and also identify how human resources interact with each other and with systems.

This view can provide a simplified representation of a pathway or network, usually depicted graphically as a connector (i.e. a line with possible amplifying information). The P2 view depicts interactions between resources that are of interest to the architect. Note that interactions between systems may be further specified in detail in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P3 Resource Connectivity' space='UPDM2P'] view.

Resources may be decomposed in the P2 view to any level (i.e. depth) of decomposition that the architect sees fit. This view may also identify the physical asset (e.g. platforms) at which resources are deployed and can show the operational nodes that utilize those resources. In many cases, an operational node depicted in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Scenario' space='UPDM2P'] view may well be the logical representation of the resource shown in the P2 view.

The P2 view may be used for:

- Definition of system concepts.
- Definition of system options.
- Human – System interactions.
- Typical Organization structures.
- Interface requirements capture.
- Capability integration planning.
- System integration management.
- Operational planning (capability configuration definition).

**Implementation**

The P2 view can be represented using:

- A P2 Resource Structure diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='P2.png']
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P2 diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Interaction in P2 diagram' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Role
- Resource Interface
- Resource Port
- Resource Connector
- Service
- Request
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575666 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01714: P3 Resource Connectivity

- page_id: `249575666`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575666/P3+Resource+Connectivity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:41:29.730+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p3-resource-connectivity']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The P3 Resource Connectivity view describes communications networks and pathways that link communications systems, and provides details regarding their configuration. The purpose of P3 view is to provide a comprehensive specification of how systems are connected, what interfaces each system exposes (ports), the hardware interface used and the protocols transmitted across the interface.

The networks and pathways documented through these views represent the physical implementation of the information needlines identified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Scenario' space='UPDM2P'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L3 Node Interactions' space='UPDM2P']view.

The P3 view focuses on the physical characteristics of each link by specifying attributes. An example of this might be the geographic location and layout of network components such as routers, switches, amplifiers and repeaters.

**Implementation**

The P3 view can be represented using:

- A P3 Resource Connectivity table. [ATTACHMENT filename='P3.PNG']
- A P3 Resource Role Connectivity table. [ATTACHMENT filename='P3_role_connectivity.png']
- A P3 spreadsheet report.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P3 table' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Exchange Element
- Resource Interaction
- Materiel
- Energy
- System
- Function
- Resource Role

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575670 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01715: P4 Resource Functions

- page_id: `249575670`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575670/P4+Resource+Functions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:41:47.200+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p4-resource-functions', 'p4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The P4 Resource Functions view addresses resource (human and non-human) functions – i.e. the activities performed by Resources.

The primary purposes of the P4 view are to:

- Develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource.
- Ensure that the functional connectivity is complete (i.e. that a resource’s required inputs are all satisfied).
- Ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail.
- Provide implementation-specific realizations of the operational activities specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L4 Logical Activities' space='UPDM2P'] view.

The Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the:

- Allocation of functions to resources.
- Flow of data between functions.

The P4 view is the systems view counterpart to the Activity Model ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L4 Logical Activities' space='UPDM2P']) of the operational view.

The P4 view may be used for:

- Description of task workflow.
- Identification of functional system requirements.
- Functional decomposition of systems.
- Relate human and system functions.

**Implementation**

The P4 view can be represented using:

- A P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram for Function hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- A P4 Resource Functions diagram for Function flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- A L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix. [ATTACHMENT filename='P4_matrix.png']
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Activity diagram.
- A SysML Block diagram.
- A SysML Activity diagram.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P4 Resource Functions diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Function
- Operational Activity
- Standard Operational Activity
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Is Capable Of Performing
- Function Action
- Function Edge
- Function Parameter
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Element</li><li>Energy</li><li>Materiel</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575678 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01716: P5 Resource States

- page_id: `249575678`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575678/P5+Resource+States
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:42:04.076+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p5-resource-states', 'p5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The P5 Resource States view describes how Resources change state in response to events and other stimuli. The P5 view identifies the states a Resource can be, the allowable changes between those states, and the triggers that cause the state changes.

The P5 view may be used to:

- Define states, events and state transitions (behavioural modelling).
- Identify constraints (input to System Requirements Document).

**Implementation**

The P5 view can be represented using a P5 Resource States diagram which is based on the UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resources State Machine
- Resource State
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575680 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01717: P6 Resource Sequence

- page_id: `249575680`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575680/P6+Resource+Sequence
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-06T07:34:30.905+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p6-resource-sequence', 'p6']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The P6 Resource Sequence view provides a time-ordered examination of the interactions between Resources. These are usually depicted as event-trace diagrams and will have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

P6 products are valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of functions and system data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or system port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, in order to perform its assigned functionality.

The P6 view may be used for:

- Analysis of resource events impacting operation.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional system requirements (input to System Requirement Document).

**Implementation**

The P6 view can be represented using a P6 Resource Sequence diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Interaction in P6 diagram' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Event Trace
- Resource Message
- Resource Role
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575683 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01718: P7 Physical Data Model

- page_id: `249575683`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575683/P7+Physical+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T07:54:41.219+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p7-physical-data-model', 'p7']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The P7 Physical Data Model view defines the structure of data used by the Resources in the architecture. It is used to describe how the information represented in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L7 Logical Data Model' space='UPDM2P']L7 Logical Data Model is implemented. While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (eg entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.

**Implementation**

The P7 view can be represented using:

- A P7 Physical Data Model diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='P7.png']

- A UML Class diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Entity Item
- Entity Attribute
- Entity Relationship
- Exchange Element
- Details
- Physical Data Model

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575686 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01719: P8 Resource Constraints

- page_id: `249575686`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575686/P8+Resource+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:43:27.658+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p8-resource-constraints', 'p8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The P8 Resource Constraints view specifies functional and non-functional constraints on the implementation aspects of the architecture (i.e. the structural and behavioral elements of the Resource viewpoint). It constraints on the resources, functions, data and ports that make up the Resource architecture. The constraints are specified in text and may be functional or structural (i.e. non-functional).

The P8 view may be used to:

- Define implementation logic.
- Identify resource constraints.

**Implementation**

The P8 view can be represented using:

- A P8 Resource Constraints table. [ATTACHMENT filename='P8.PNG']

- A P8 spreadsheet report.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P8 table' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Constraint
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Function
- Entity Item
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

##### Description

UPDM: A type of a human being that is recognized by law as the subject of rights and duties. This is used to define the characteristics that require capturing for ActualPersons (e.g., properties such as address, rank, telephone number, etc.).

MODAF/NAF: NA

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575877 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01724: Physical Data Model

- page_id: `249575877`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575877/Physical+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:39:55.655+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Systems Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

476178045

476178048

476178046

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A PhysicalDataModel is an implementable specification of a data structure. A PhysicalDataModel realizes a LogicalDataModel, taking into account implementation restrictions and performance issues whilst still enforcing the constraints, relationships and typing of the logical model.

DoDAF: A Physical Data Model defines the structure of the various kinds of system or service data that are utilized by the systems or services in the Architecture.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Package

##### Related DoDAF views

[DIV-3 Physical Data Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/DIV-3+Physical+Data+Model)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SV-11 Physical Schema](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/MODAF.+SV-11+Physical+Schema)

##### Related NAF views

[NSV-11 Systems Data Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/NSV-11+Systems+Data+Model)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575644 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01725: Physical Resource viewpoint

- page_id: `249575644`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575644/Physical+Resource+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-13T10:12:32.232+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['physical-resource-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Physical Resource viewpoint is a set of graphical and textual products that describes systems and interconnections providing for or supporting NATO processes. This viewpoint associates systems resources to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logical viewpoint' space='UPDM2P'] and/or the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service viewpoint' space='UPDM2P']. These systems are the resources that are used to construct the services of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service viewpoint' space='UPDM2P'], and support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information among operational nodes as defined in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logical viewpoint' space='UPDM2P'].

#### PANEL: Views

Views

1

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

These systems are the resources that are used to construct the services of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Service viewpoint" /></ac:link>, and support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information among operational nodes as defined in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Logical viewpoint" /></ac:link>.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Views</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children"><ac:parameter ac:name="depth">1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 01726: Post

- page_id: `249575837`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575837/Post
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:12:56.568+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Operational Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1100808206

1100808208

1100808207

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A Post (MODAF::PostType, NAF::PostType) is a type of point of contact or responsible person. Note that this is the type of post, e.g., Desk Officer, Commander Land Component, etc.

DoDAF: A Post (DoDAF::PersonType) is a category of persons defined by the role or roles they share that are relevant to an architecture.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575689 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01727: Pr Configuration Management

- page_id: `249575689`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575689/Pr+Configuration+Management
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-17T08:38:20.362+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['pr-configuration-management', 'pr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Pr Configuration Management view depicts the whole lifecycle view of a Resource, describing how its configuration changes over time. The Pr view provides an overview of how a capability configuration structure changes over time. It shows the structure of different versions of Resources (usually Capability Configurations or Service Implementations) mapped against a timeline.

The Pr view may be used for:

- Development of incremental acquisition strategy.
- Planning technology insertion.

**Implementation**

The Pr view can be represented using:

- A Pr Configuration Management diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- A Pr MS Word document report.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Pr diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Version of Configurations in Pr diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Resources in Pr diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing Resources from Pr diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Version of Configurations in Pr diagram' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Whole Life Configuration
- Version Of Configuration
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Actual Project Milestone
- Increment Milestone
- Out Of Service Milestone

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The Pr view provides an overview of how a capability configuration structure changes over time. It shows the structure of different versions of Resources (usually Capability Configurations or Service Implementations) mapped against a timeline.</p><p>The Pr view may be used for:</p><ul><li>Development of incremental acquisition strategy.</li><li>Planning technology insertion.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Pr view can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A Pr Configuration Management diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:alt="Pr Configuration Management diagram" ac:title="Pr Configuration Management diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Pr.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Pr Configuration Management" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ul><li>A Pr MS Word document report.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="bfbebdf2-8a6d-498f-ba66-7daf78664a20" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576097 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01728: Predefined Metric Suites

- page_id: `249576097`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576097/Predefined+Metric+Suites
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-27T14:22:36.390+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Coverage Analysis
- labels: ['metric-suites', 'capabilities-covered-by-nodes', 'capabilities-covered-by-opactivities', 'nodes-covered-by-sysresources', 'opactivitie-covered-by-functions', 'opactivities-covered-by-nodes', 'capabilities-covered-by-sysinterfaces', 'capabilities-covered-by-capconfiguration', 'capabilities-covered-by-projects', 'orgresources-covered-by-projects', 'projects-covered-by-milestones', 'functions-covered-by-sysresources']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Metric suite is a collection of parameters and metric definitions used to calculate metrics that evaluate specific aspects of your model.

For your convenience, there are eleven predefined metric suites. See the following figure for better understanding of the predefined metric suites and their purposes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Capabilities Covered by Nodes**

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by Nodes" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Nodes in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Nodes, and the coverage average are also counted.

**Capabilities Covered by OpActivities**

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by OpActivities" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Operational Activities in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Operational Activities, and the coverage average are also counted.

**Nodes Covered by SysResources**

The Metric Suite "Nodes Covered by SysResources" counts the percentage of the Nodes covered by System Resources in the particular package. The number of Nodes, System Resources, and the coverage average are also counted.

**OpActivities Covered by Functions**

The Metric Suite "OpActivities Covered by Functions" counts the percentage of the Operational Activities covered by Functions in the particular package. The number of Operational Activities, Functions, and the coverage average are also counted.

**OpActivities Covered by Nodes**

The Metric Suite "OpActivities Covered by Nodes" counts the percentage of the Operational Activities covered by Nodes in the particular package. The number of Operational Activities, Nodes, and the coverage average are also counted.

**Capabilities Covered by SysInterfaces**

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by SysInterfaces" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by System Interfaces in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, System Interfaces, and the coverage average are also counted.

**Capabilities Covered by CapConfiguration**

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by CapConfiguration" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Capability Configurations or Physical Architectures in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Capability Configurations or Physical Architectures, and the coverage average are also counted.

**Capabilities Covered by Projects**

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by Projects" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by the Actual Projects in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Actual Projects, and the coverage average are also counted.

**OrgResources Covered by Projects**

The Metric Suite "OrgResources Covered by Projects" counts the percentage of the Organization Resources covered by the Actual Projects in the particular package. The number of Organization Resources, Actual Projects, and the coverage average are also counted.

****

**Projects Covered by Milestones**

The Metric Suite "Projects Covered by Milestones" counts the percentage of the Actual Projects covered by the Actual Milestones in the particular package. The number of Actual Projects, Actual Milestones, and the coverage average are also counted.

****

**Functions Covered by SysResources**

The Metric Suite "Functions Covered by SysResources" counts the percentage of the Functions covered by the System Resources in the particular package. The number of Functions, System Resources, and the coverage average are also counted

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A Metric suite is a collection of parameters and metric definitions used to calculate metrics that evaluate specific aspects of your model.</p><p>For your convenience, there are eleven predefined metric suites. See the following figure for better understanding of the predefined metric suites and their purposes.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Metric Suite hierarchy" ac:title="Metric Suite hierarchy"><ri:attachment ri:filename="metric_suite_hierarchy.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Predefined Metric Suites" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><strong>Capabilities Covered by Nodes</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by Nodes&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Nodes in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Nodes, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Capabilities Covered by OpActivities</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by OpActivities&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Operational Activities in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Operational Activities, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Nodes Covered by SysResources</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Nodes Covered by SysResources&quot; counts the percentage of the Nodes covered by System Resources in the particular package. The number of Nodes, System Resources, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>OpActivities Covered by Functions</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;OpActivities Covered by Functions&quot; counts the percentage of the Operational Activities covered by Functions in the particular package. The number of Operational Activities, Functions, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>OpActivities Covered by Nodes</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;OpActivities Covered by Nodes&quot; counts the percentage of the Operational Activities covered by Nodes in the particular package. The number of Operational Activities, Nodes, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Capabilities Covered by SysInterfaces</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by SysInterfaces&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by System Interfaces in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, System Interfaces, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Capabilities Covered by CapConfiguration</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by CapConfiguration&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Capability Configurations or Physical Architectures in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Capability Configurations or Physical Architectures, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Capabilities Covered by Projects</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by Projects&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by the Actual Projects in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Actual Projects, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>OrgResources Covered by Projects</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;OrgResources Covered by Projects&quot; counts the percentage of the Organization Resources covered by the Actual Projects in the particular package. The number of Organization Resources, Actual Projects, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Projects Covered by Milestones</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Projects Covered by Milestones&quot; counts the percentage of the Actual Projects covered by the Actual Milestones in the particular package. The number of Actual Projects, Actual Milestones, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Functions Covered by SysResources</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Functions Covered by SysResources&quot; counts the percentage of the Functions covered by the System Resources in the particular package. The number of Functions, System Resources, and the coverage average are also counted</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575724 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01729: Predefined Relation Maps

- page_id: `249575724`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575724/Predefined+Relation+Maps
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-05-15T22:02:19.290+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Supportive Views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Relation Map diagram enables you to review and analyze relations rapidly between the elements of the whole model. Our modeling tool presents you a number of predefined relation maps. You will be able to change the context and scope only. You can change the context by dragging the selected element onto the map.

The predefined relation maps are as follows:

##### Capability Structural Map

A Capability Structural Map depicts the structural relationships of a Capability. The predefined map includes:

- the Capability itself
- related DoDAF Performers (or MODAF Nodes)
- related concrete System Resources including Capability Configurations, Organization Types, Software System Resources

A Capability Structural Map also presents the internal compositions of each mapped element as well as the relationships among these elements.

##### Capability Behavioral Map

A Capability Behavioral Map depicts the behavioral relationships of a Capability. The predefined map includes:

- the Capability itself
- related Operational Activities
- related System Functions

A Capability Behavioral Map also presents the internal decomposition of each mapped behavioral element as well as the relationships among these elements.

##### Implementation Map

The Implementation Map depicts the implementation relationships of a DoDAF Performer (or of a MODAF Node). The predefined map includes either the DoDAF Performer (or the MODAF Node) and the concrete System Resource(s) that has (have) been indicated as implementing that operational element. For example, the Capability Configuration, Organization Type, or Software element that implements the Performer or Node. When the Performer or Node has composite structure and when the System Resources have composite structure, the Implementation Map also includes the implementation elements for the composed elements.

##### Operational Activity Implementation Map

The Operational Activity Implementation Map depicts the behavioral implementation of an Operational Activity, relating the Operational Activity and the Systems Functions that implement the operational behavior. When the Operational Activity has composite behaviors and when the System Functions have composite functionality, the Operational Activity Implementation Map also includes the behavioral elements for the composed elements.

##### Operational Activity Map

The Operational Activity Map depicts the behavioral decomposition of an Operational Activity.

##### Organization Structure Map

The Organization Structure Map depicts the structural composition of an Actual Organization. The predefined map includes: the Actual Organization itself, DoDAF Individual Person Roles (or MODAF Actual Posts), and Actual Person elements. The Organization Structure Map illustrates the relationships between these organizational elements.

##### System Resource Map

The System Resource Map depicts the structural decomposition of a concrete System Resource-such as a Capability Configuration, Organization Type, or Software-element.

##### Variant Map

The Variant Map depicts concrete specializations of an abstract System Resource such as the direct specializations Service Access, Capability Configuration, and Software and any other specializations of these that appear in the modeled generalization hierarchy.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Relation Map diagram enables you to review and analyze relations rapidly between the elements of the whole model. Our modeling tool presents you a number of predefined relation maps. You will be able to change the context and scope only. You can change the context by dragging the selected element onto the map.</p><p>The predefined relation maps are as follows:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f2c7cce1-4f9b-4604-9e6e-3ac3b551926b" /></p><h3>Capability Structural Map</h3><p><span>A Capability Structural Map depicts the structural relationships of a Capability. The predefined map includes:</span></p><ul><li>the Capability itself</li><li>related DoDAF Performers (or MODAF Nodes)</li><li><span>related concrete System Resources including Capability Configurations, Organization Types, </span>Software System Resources</li></ul><p><span>A Capability Structural Map also presents the internal compositions of each mapped element as well as the relationships among these elements.</span></p><h3>Capability Behavioral Map</h3><p><span>A Capability Behavioral Map depicts the behavioral relationships of a Capability. The predefined map includes:</span></p><ul><li><span>the Capability itself</span></li><li>related Operational Activities</li><li>related System Functions</li></ul><p><span>A Capability Behavioral Map also presents the internal decomposition of each mapped behavioral element as well as the relationships among these elements.</span></p><h3>Implementation Map</h3><p><span>The Implementation Map depicts the implementation relationships of a DoDAF Performer (or of a MODAF Node). The predefined map includes either the DoDAF Performer (or the MODAF Node) and the concrete System Resource(s) that has (have) been indicated as implementing that operational element. For example, the Capability Configuration, Organization Type, or Software element that implements the Performer or Node. When the Performer or Node has composite structure and when the System Resources have composite structure, the Implementation Map also includes the implementation elements for the composed elements.</span></p><h3>Operational Activity Implementation Map</h3><p><span>The Operational Activity Implementation Map depicts the behavioral implementation of an Operational Activity, relating the Operational Activity and the Systems Functions that implement the operational behavior. When the Operational Activity has composite behaviors and when the System Functions have composite functionality, the Operational Activity Implementation Map also includes the behavioral elements for the composed elements.</span></p><h3>Operational Activity Map</h3><p><span>The Operational Activity Map depicts the behavioral decomposition of an Operational Activity.</span></p><h3>Organization Structure Map</h3><p><span>The Organization Structure Map depicts the structural composition of an Actual Organization. The predefined map includes: the Actual Organization itself, DoDAF Individual Person Roles (or MODAF Actual Posts), and Actual Person elements. The Organization Structure Map illustrates the relationships between these organizational elements.</span></p><h3>System Resource Map</h3><p><span>The System Resource Map depicts the structural decomposition of a concrete System Resource-such as a Capability Configuration, Organization Type, or Software-element.</span></p><h3>Variant Map</h3><p><span>The Variant Map depicts concrete specializations of an abstract System Resource such as the direct specializations Service Access, Capability Configuration, and Software and any other specializations of these that appear in the modeled generalization hierarchy.</span></p><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 01730: Problem Domain

- page_id: `249575838`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575838/Problem+Domain
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:26:06.845+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Operational Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1100860662

1100860664

1100860663

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: The boundary containing those Nodes which may be realized by functional resources specified in SV-1. There may be more than one alternative solution for a given ProblemDomain specified as a set of SV suites. There may be only one ProblemDomain in a LogicalArchitecture.

DoDAF: NA – covered by the more general temporalWholePart element.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Property

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. OV-2 Operational Node Relationship Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/MODAF.+OV-2+Operational+Node+Relationship+Description)

##### Related NAF views

[NOV-2 Operational Node Connectivity Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/NOV-2+Operational+Node+Connectivity+Description)
